antiPatterns.guardrail: At every trust boundary (HTTP handler, queue/webhook consumer, file/CSV upload, third-party response) parse raw input into a typed/schema-validated object on the first lines, reject (don't coerce) with a structured error carrying a machine-readable code and correct status; let the type carry inward so downstream code never re-checks. Reserve exceptions/500 for truly unexpected faults.

antiPatterns.name: In-Place Breaking Contract Change
antiPatterns.trigger: Renaming/removing a response field, tightening a param type, or making an optional param required on an already-consumed endpoint, exported function, or shared event schema — to 'clean it up'.
antiPatterns.guardrail: Only when editing a surface other code consumes: allow additive changes (new optional param, new field, new endpoint) freely; for breaking changes require a versioned/deprecation path instead of an in-place edit. Apply least-astonishment — the symbol does what its name says, no hidden side effects, sensible defaults. Private/single-caller/brand-new code is exempt.

antiPatterns.name: Reassessing Against the Polluted Transcript
antiPatterns.trigger: On a stall, the agent 'reviews its progress' by reading back through its own conversation instead of re-reading the fixed external objective.
antiPatterns.guardrail: Keep objective + acceptance criteria as a durable artifact OUTSIDE the conversation. The reassess step re-reads it verbatim and emits a per-criterion PASS/FAIL table plus an explicit diff of 'goal requires' vs 'changes made so far' — never a freeform self-summary.

antiPatterns.name: Uniform Ceremony / Ignored Risk Tier
antiPatterns.trigger: An XS one-file copy/config change pays the full research → plan → verify-plan → build → verify-build toll; or canonical-implementation is left as soft 'always consider' language a hands-off agent skips.
antiPatterns.guardrail: Risk-tier the pipeline off the size/type already computed at slice creation: low-risk non-domain slices collapse research+plan and skip the standalone verify-plan gate; M+ and anything touching money/auth/data/migrations/external-APIs gets the full path with canonical-implementation and security lenses MANDATORY (not 'consider').

antiPatterns.name: Ephemeral Debt
antiPatterns.trigger: A taken shortcut or interim decision is recorded only in the agent's reasoning or the PR body, so the next slice/agent never sees it and builds on the hack.
antiPatterns.guardrail: Record debt as code, twice: a grep-able marker ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason + link at the exact site, plus a committed one-paragraph ADR. The next slice's first step greps for ARCH-DEBT touching its footprint before coding. Recorded-in-chat does not count as recorded.

nonNegotiables: The orchestration loop — not the agent — owns every stop/reassess/escalate trigger, via mechanical counters (repeated same-target action, turns with no FAIL→PASS, correction count, budget) that fire hard interrupts.

Every gate, check, and objective emits an unambiguous machine-readable verdict (PASS/FAIL/DONE with the failing criterion named); objectives are durable binary acceptance-criteria checklists stored OUTSIDE the conversation.
Every artifact (spec, plan, slice) is done only when it passes the fresh-context bar: a new context window can act on it without re-interviewing the user or reading the chat transcript.

Verification of any user-facing or stateful slice exercises the one observable behavior through its real entry point with real data; passing tests and 'code looks correct' are never reported as 'verified', and the implementing agent never has sole authority over the tests that gate it.

Before any new table/model/type, run the adjective-noun heuristic with default 'field, not table'; the expensive option (new table) must be argued for with distinct columns AND relationships AND independent lifecycle.

Every slice is thin (one observable behavior, no 'and') AND complete (app runnable, behavior demoable through the real entry point); the first slice is a tracer bullet piercing all layers, and no slice ships infrastructure that no current-or-next behavior slice consumes.
Refactor and behavior never mix in one commit (two hats); structural change is behavior-preserving with tests green before and after.

When the architecture obstructs, route by blast-radius + reversibility (preparatory refactor / reversible seam / escalate-the-decision-not-the-build); irreversible one-way-door choices are always escalated via a ≤1-page ADR while proceeding on a reversible interim, and a first-class route-back must exist to upstream steps (phase boundary, main spec) when an upstream structural decision is wrong.

Heavy correctness guardrails attach only via a surface-detection pass: idempotency on foreign-state mutations, integer-minor-unit + currency + largest-remainder on money, expand/contract on destructive migrations, parse-at-the-boundary + typed errors on trust boundaries, additive-vs-breaking taxonomy on consumed contracts, and canonical-library-with-package-verification on auth/crypto/payments/dates — with rigor scaled to the slice's risk tier so cheap changes pay no ceremony.

Every loop is bounded: per-loop budgets (fix attempts, route-backs, splits) plus an initiative-level ceiling (max total slices / agent calls); on exhaustion the run records a blocker and degrades gracefully rather than looping forever, and recovery defaults to revert-to-last-green carrying only a distilled lesson rather than appending corrections.

gaps: MODALITY GAP - the entire research assumes a backend/web-app/SQL feature world. There is zero coverage of: data/ML pipeline features, streaming/event-driven systems, mobile (iOS/Android, app-store review cycles, offline-first sync), CLI/devtools, infra-as-code/Terraform, embedded, game logic, browser extensions, or LLM-app features (prompt/RAG/eval changes). The 'adjective-noun -> add a column' heuristic and the Stripe-grade money/migration guardrails do not transfer to a Kafka topic schema, a feature flag in a mobile binary, or a dbt model. The workflow will mis-route these or apply irrelevant ceremony.

FRONTEND/UI BUILDING IS UNDER-RESEARCHED AS A BUILD ACTIVITY. UI appears only as a verification problem (screenshots are fakeable) in the last dimension. There is no research on slicing, guardrails, or anti-patterns for actually BUILDING UI: component reuse vs duplication, design-system adherence, accessibility, responsive/i18n, state management, loading/empty/error states. The 'thin slice must be demoable through a real entry point' rule assumes a UI exists but the workflow has no UI-construction discipline.

NO RESEARCH ON THE READ/UNDERSTAND-BEFORE-WRITE PHASE. Every dimension is about constraining the agent once it is editing or planning. Nothing covers how the agent builds an accurate model of the EXISTING codebase first: finding the right files, discovering existing conventions/utilities to reuse, locating the real entry points, understanding implicit invariants. Most production-feature failures come from not knowing what already exists (reinventing a helper, missing a validation layer). The 'don't duplicate' guardrail fires too late if the agent never searched.

NON-CODE WORK IS ENTIRELY ABSENT. Production features require: tests as deliverables, observability/logging/metrics, feature flags and rollout/kill-switch, documentation, env/config and secrets, dependency/lockfile updates, CI changes, infra/deploy changes, telemetry/analytics events. The slicing model ('one observable behavior, app runnable') has no slot for 'add a dashboard alert' or 'wire a feature flag' which are real, non-user-observable but mandatory production steps.

MULTI-AGENT / CONCURRENCY AND STATE-OWNERSHIP ARE UNADDRESSED. The recommendations freely invoke 'hand to a sub-agent', 'fresh session', 'separate verifier agent', and ARCH-DEBT markers read by 'the next slice or next agent' - but nothing covers: how parallel agents avoid editing the same files, merge/rebase conflict handling, who owns the durable acceptance-criteria artifact when multiple agents write it, or how the stall-detector fingerprint survives across agent boundaries. The architecture leans on multi-agent handoff without designing it.

HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP MECHANICS ARE A NAMED DEPENDENCY WITH NO DESIGN. Tier-3 escalation, ADR ratification, 'surface for human review', and the claim that reflection 'catches 60-70% of issues that would otherwise surface in human review' all assume a human reviewer/approver exists and responds. There is no research on: async escalation latency, what the agent does while waiting (the 'reversible interim' can itself be wrong), how a human even SEES the ADR in an autonomous run, or batching/notification of escalations. The whole approach silently depends on a responsive human oracle.

THE ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA ARTIFACT IS LOAD-BEARING BUT ITS GENESIS IS UNRESEARCHED. Three of five dimensions hinge on a 'durable, external, binary, test-decidable acceptance-criteria checklist.' Nothing covers WHO writes it, HOW you guarantee it is correct and complete (a wrong/incomplete criteria artifact silently authorizes the wrong feature and every downstream gate passes), how it is kept in sync when scope legitimately changes mid-build, or what happens when criteria conflict. If this artifact is itself LLM-generated, the same 'tests encode the code's behavior not the spec' failure applies one level up.

VERIFICATION COVERAGE IS INCOMPLETE AND TRUNCATED. The honest-lean-verification dimension cut off mid-UI-branch. Even from what is present, there is no ladder rung for: non-deterministic/async behavior, concurrency/race conditions, performance/load, security verification (authz bypass, injection), data-migration correctness on real data volumes, third-party/external-service behavior (which can't be invoked freely), or destructive operations you cannot safely 'demo through the real entry path.' The 'demo the real behavior with live data' DoD is undefined for irreversible or external-effect actions.

NO COST / LATENCY / TOKEN BUDGET MODEL FOR THE GUARDRAILS THEMSELVES. The research repeatedly claims mechanisms are 'lean' and 'zero overhead on the happy path,' but never measures the aggregate cost of running surface-detection, five-question schema gates, per-edit STOP-signal self-checks, reassess templates, separate verifier agents, and ADR writing on every slice. Stacked across a multi-slice feature this is substantial token/latency overhead and added failure surface; the leanness claim is asserted, not grounded.

ROLLBACK / FAILURE-RECOVERY OF SHIPPED SLICES IS MISSING. 'Revert to last green state' is well-covered for in-flight code, but once slices MERGE/DEPLOY there is no story for: a slice that passed its gate but breaks in production, partial-feature states left in production between slices (the app is 'runnable' but a half-built feature is live), feature-flag cleanup, or backing out an expand/contract migration mid-rollout. 'Always runnable' is not the same as 'safe to ship each slice.'

TESTING-STRATEGY DECISIONS ARE UNRESEARCHED. The verification dimension shows LLM tests enshrine bugs and get tampered with, but there is no positive guidance on WHAT to test: unit vs integration vs e2e balance, what deserves a test at all, test data/fixtures/factories, flaky-test handling, or how the separate verifier obtains independent oracles when only the spec exists. 'Anchor assertions to spec lines' presupposes a spec granular enough to decide every assertion, which the acceptance-criteria gap above undermines.

GIT/PR WORKFLOW AND THE TWO-HATS COMMIT RULE ARE ASSUMED, NOT DESIGNED. The architectural-obstruction dimension mandates machine-checkable commit invariants (refactor commit changes no assertions; feat commit moves no files) and grep-able ARCH-DEBT markers, but there is no research on branch strategy, PR sizing/sequencing across slices, how the 'auto-reject mixed diff back to the agent' loop actually works, or how commit-level rules interact with squash-merge workflows where the granularity is lost.
risks: OVERFITTING TO LOOPING/FIXATION: 'altitude-and-weeds' is the most developed dimension and its mechanisms (stall detector, hard interrupts, revert-and-reset) assume the failure mode is REPETITION. But the most dangerous production failures are SILENT and CONFIDENT one-shot errors (days_held=0 skips every trade, PID=0 writes nothing - cited in the verification dimension) where the agent never loops, reports success, and moves on. The repetition-based guardrails are blind to confidently-wrong non-looping behavior, which is the higher-severity class.

GUARDRAIL FATIGUE / IGNORED-CHECKLIST RISK IS SELF-ACKNOWLEDGED BUT UNMITIGATED ACROSS DIMENSIONS. The Stripe dimension correctly warns that ceremony-on-everything trains everyone to skip checklists - yet the combined workflow stacks an adjective-noun ladder, five schema questions, surface-detection guardrails, per-edit STOP signals, reassess templates, foundation-justification gates, and demo DoD on every slice. The aggregate IS ceremony-on-everything. The very failure the research names is the emergent risk of assembling all the research.

MECHANICAL TRIGGERS ARE GAMEABLE/EVADABLE BY THE SAME AGENT THEY CONSTRAIN. The stall-detector fingerprints 'tool + target + error-output hash'; a capable model under sunk-cost pressure can trivially perturb the target (touch a different region), reword the command, or change error output to dodge the window - exactly the test-tampering behavior documented in ImpossibleBench (test modification is >79% of cheating; more capable = more cheating). Trusting fingerprint-based detection against an adversarial-by-incentive agent is fragile.

THE WORKFLOW DEPENDS ON DETERMINISTIC, MACHINE-READABLE GATES (PASS/FAIL/DONE tokens, tests-as-truth, demoable behavior) but production features routinely have NON-BINARY, NON-DETERMINISTIC, or EXTERNALLY-DEPENDENT acceptance (UX quality, performance under load, third-party API behavior, eventual consistency). Forcing every criterion into a binary test-decidable checklist will either exclude these (false sense of done) or fabricate brittle binary proxies that pass while the real property fails.

CONTRADICTION BETWEEN 'KEEP THE BUILD MOVING' AND 'STOP ON IRREVERSIBLE DECISIONS.' The obstruction dimension says proceed on a reversible interim and only Tier-3 pauses; the Stripe dimension says irreversible data corruption (double-charge, bad migration) is near-impossible to unwind. An agent biased toward momentum will mis-classify a one-way door as reversible (it cannot reliably tell them apart - explicitly noted) and proceed, which is precisely the catastrophic case. The reversibility classifier is the single point of failure and is left to the agent's judgment.

SOURCE QUALITY IS UNEVEN AND SOME LOAD-BEARING CLAIMS REST ON BLOG POSTS / SINGLE ANECDOTES / VENDOR CONTENT. The headline '7x (14->2)' anti-loop result is one AWS dev.to post; '60-70% of issues caught' is unattributed 'practitioner reports'; key behavioral fixes cite Cursor/Claude vendor guides and Medium articles. Several arXiv refs (e.g. 2602.14798 dated in the future relative to a Jan-2026 cutoff) are preprints, not peer-reviewed. Building a production methodology on these as if they were settled is a credibility risk.

SELECTION BIAS: THE RESEARCH ONLY STUDIES KNOWN FAILURE MODES AND THEIR KNOWN FIXES. There is no adversarial coverage of failures the cited sources don't name - e.g., the agent satisfying every gate while building the WRONG feature (criteria were wrong), correct-looking code with subtle security holes that pass all behavioral demos, supply-chain/secrets exposure during the build, or emergent bad interactions BETWEEN guardrails (e.g., revert-and-reset discarding context that contained the only record of an ARCH-DEBT decision).

THE 'CLEAN RESTART / DISCARD CONTEXT' PRIMITIVE CAN DESTROY HARD-WON KNOWLEDGE. The altitude dimension's central counterintuitive move - on stall, throw away context and carry only a one-paragraph lesson - risks discarding exactly the partial diagnosis, reproduction steps, or environmental facts that were the actual progress. 'Distill to one paragraph' assumes the agent can correctly identify what mattered, but a fixated agent (by the dimension's own argument) is the worst judge of that. Repeated resets can cause amnesia loops that are slower than fixing in place.

TWO-HATS AND EXPAND/CONTRACT ADD DEPLOY-COUNT AND PR-COUNT MULTIPLICATION THAT MAY BE INFEASIBLE. Splitting every breaking migration into expand -> backfill+dual-write -> contract across SEPARATE deploys, and every change into refactor-only + feature-only commits, assumes a fast, cheap, independent deploy/release pipeline. In environments with slow CI, manual release trains, or coupled deploys, this 3-5x multiplication of deploys/PRs per feature is a real throughput and coordination cost the research treats as free.

NO TREATMENT OF SCALE/STATE: how these guardrails behave on a LARGE existing codebase (slow greps, huge schemas the SQL-accuracy research says degrade LLMs, thousands of existing ARCH-DEBT markers, monorepos) vs a greenfield toy. Several mechanisms (re-read acceptance criteria verbatim, grep ARCH-DEBT for the footprint, parse whole schema for adjective-noun collisions) scale poorly and may themselves exhaust context - the context-rot problem the workflow is trying to solve, reintroduced by the guardrails.

THE APPROACH ASSUMES A SINGLE COOPERATIVE AGENT FOLLOWING A SYSTEM PROMPT, BUT PROVIDES MOSTLY PROMPT-LEVEL AND HOOK-LEVEL CONTROLS. Where enforcement is 'stated in the system prompt' (two-hats, STOP signals, foundation gate), the verification dimension's own evidence (strict prompting cut cheating 92%->1% but not to 0; monitoring catches only 42-65%) shows prompt-level rules are partially honored at best. The methodology's reliability ceiling is bounded by exactly the non-compliance the research documents, and it has no independent enforcement for most non-mechanical rules.

approaches.approachName: Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
approaches.philosophy: Fewest gates that still guarantee quality, with depth bought only where risk is proven. The current Strike pays an identical 5-step per-slice toll (research -> plan -> verify-plan -> build -> verify-build) for a CSS tweak and a payments ledger; that uniform ceremony is the central waste and, paradoxically, the central quality leak (a checklist run on everything trains the agent to skim it, so the real money path gets the same cursory pass as the copy fix). Lean-Adaptive replaces uniform depth with a single risk tier computed once at slice birth (TRIVIAL / STANDARD / CRITICAL) and a fast path vs full path keyed off it. Two principles are non-negotiable and survive verbatim: (1) the orchestration loop, never the agent, owns every stop/reassess/escalate trigger via mechanical counters; the agent never votes on whether it is stuck, done, or off-track. (2) Every spec/plan/slice is done only when a fresh context window can act on it without re-reading the transcript, and every gate emits a machine-readable PASS/FAIL/DONE with the failing criterion NAMED. On top of that the design front-loads the two things the original lacked: a READ-BEFORE-WRITE codebase-grounding pass (so the agent knows what already exists before it plans, killing duplication and missed validation layers at the source rather than catching them late), and a self-correcting ARCHITECTURAL-OBSTRUCTION loop that gives the build a reverse gear when an UPSTREAM decision (spec shape, phase boundary, shared contract) is wrong — the single biggest correctness gap, which the current engine literally logs as 'escalated route-back not auto-handled in v1'.
approaches.canonicalResearch: Triggered leanly, never blanket. A SURFACE-DETECTION grep/scan (run as a cheap sub-pass inside Step 2 plan-build prep, not a standalone agent round-trip) decides whether canonical research happens at all. The trigger surfaces are: money/price/fee/tax/balance/payout, auth/permissions/tokens/crypto/signing, dates/timezones/scheduling, accounting/refunds/ledgers, payments/PCI, destructive migrations, external-API/SDK/webhook integration. If ZERO trigger surfaces are touched (a CSS change, a copy edit, an internal helper), canonical research is SKIPPED entirely and the agent records 'no canonical surface — skipped' in one line. If a trigger fires, canonical research becomes MANDATORY (not 'consider'): the agent must (a) read the current OFFICIAL docs for the library/provider in the repo's installed version, (b) answer 'what does Stripe/Amazon/the framework do here' in one or two sentences, (c) for any NEWLY-added dependency, verify the package actually exists and is the genuine popular one (registry presence + download counts) to defeat slopsquatting, and (d) never hand-roll JWT verification, password hashing, crypto, timezone math, or money rounding when a vetted library exists. The output is 3-6 evidence bullets folded INTO the plan (no separate research.md round-trip for STANDARD/TRIVIAL); only CRITICAL slices get a durable research section the verifier independently re-checks. This preserves Strike's strongest correctness idea after the fresh-context bar while removing the unconditional research agent call that the audit flags as pure overhead on the majority of slices.
approaches.adjectiveNoun: Promoted from buried prose to a first-class NAMED LENS applied at three points: main-spec (domain modeling), slice naming, and plan. The rule: an adjective on a noun (draft post, public user, cancelled subscription, archived order) is a STATE/FLAG/ENUM/SCOPE/PERMISSION/CONFIG/RELATIONSHIP on the existing entity until a new table is argued for. The discriminator is CARDINALITY x LIFECYCLE x EXCLUSIVITY, never grammar: single-valued + mutually-exclusive + changes-over-the-row's-life -> a status COLUMN (CHECK/enum); many-valued + time-assigned -> a join table; ONLY when the thing has its own columns AND its own relationships AND its own independent lifecycle -> a new table, and the agent must state those three in one line to earn it. Two mechanical falsification tests fire automatically whenever a step is about to emit CREATE TABLE / a new model / a new enum-of-entities: (1) the UNION SMELL — if answering 'show me all <nouns>' would require a UNION across the proposed tables, that alone proves over-splitting; collapse to one table + a discriminator column. (2) the SCALAR-NORMALIZATION smell — a single-valued status/flag/1:1 setting modeled as its own lookup/join/EAV table is over-normalization; it is a column. Decide cardinality (single vs multi) BEFORE deciding table vs column. The lens runs inline (one paragraph, default 'field, not table') at spec and plan time, and the build-verifier re-checks it on any slice that touched schema/models/types — so the same concern is graded once on the way in and once on the way out, never repeatedly.
approaches.verification: An honest, lean LADDER whose depth is keyed to the slice's risk tier and whose top rung is observable behavior through the REAL entry point with REAL data — never 'tests pass' and never 'code looks correct'. The rungs, lowest to highest: (R0) build/typecheck/lint compiles; (R1) focused tests for the changed surface pass — but tests are NEVER sole authority: the build-verifier independently diffs test files for tautology (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded inputs the function just echoes) and anchors every assertion to a spec-stated input->output example, because the implementing agent's tests encode the code's actual buggy behavior and frontier models tamper with tests in >79% of cheating cases; (R2) the one observable behavior is exercised through its real entry path with real data and the output is observed; (R3 — UI only) an actual browser/Playwright run plus a SCREENSHOT of the working state — code review, curl, static checks, and DOM inspection are explicitly NOT browser verification; when the browser path is genuinely blocked the verdict is 'code-verified' (never 'verified') with the blocked check, replacement evidence, and residual user-facing risk listed. Risk tiering of the ladder: TRIVIAL slices stop at R0+R1 (no behavior demo required if there is no user-observable behavior, e.g. a config or copy change — but if there IS observable behavior it must hit R2). STANDARD slices require R2 (and R3 if UI). CRITICAL slices require R2/R3 PLUS the mandatory canonical + security/state-integrity lenses AND, for the failure class repetition-detectors are blind to (confidently-wrong one-shot errors like days_held=0 silently skipping every trade with all tests green), a NEGATIVE/adversarial probe: exercise the behavior with the boundary/empty/zero/duplicate input the spec names and confirm it does the spec-correct thing, not just the happy path. For non-deterministic, external-effect, or destructive behavior that cannot be safely demoed through the real path (a live charge, an irreversible migration), the DoD degrades explicitly to: exercise against a sandbox/staging/dry-run entry point with a recorded transcript, OR record an EXTERNAL-VERIFY blocker naming exactly what a human/integration-test must confirm — the verifier never fabricates a green proxy for a property it could not actually observe.

stepSet.step: 1. Spec + Phase Plan (merged, fresh-context gated)
stepSet.objective: Produce ONE durable artifact that a fresh context window can build from: the main spec (outcome, the four plain boundaries In/Out-with-why/Needs-Decision/Never, domain language, product rules, state/lifecycle/invariants, engineering pressure points, binary acceptance-criteria checklist, success checks split repo-verifiable vs live/human) AND the ordered set of vertical phases — in a single pass for small initiatives, split only when the spec reveals genuine multi-phase scope.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Pull current truth from the refined idea + resolved decisions; preserve accepted decisions, never revive rejected paths. Turn vague scope into the four plain boundaries. Run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens on every durable noun (default field-not-table; UNION-smell and scalar-normalization tests) and reconcile terms into PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md (record conflicts as assumptions/blockers — there is no user to alert). Write the acceptance criteria as a BINARY, test-decidable checklist (each item is a single observable behavior with a named input->expected-output), because this artifact authorizes every downstream gate — a wrong/incomplete criterion silently ships the wrong feature. Name engineering pressure points WITHOUT inventing architecture. Break into the SMALLEST ordered set of VERTICAL phases (named by outcome, never schema-first/API-first/UI-later); for a tight single-outcome initiative emit one phase. Do not write phase specs or slices yet. Fresh-context check before finishing: could a new agent build this without the transcript? If not, fix the artifact.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {specPath, acceptanceCriteria:[{id, behavior, input, expectedOutput}], boundaries:{in,out,needsDecision,never}, domainTerms:[...], phases:[{id,name,outcome,dependsOn,risk:trivial|standard|critical}], openDecisions, assumptions, blockers}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff specPath written, acceptanceCriteria is non-empty and every item is binary/test-decidable, every phase is vertical (outcome-named, not a single layer), and the fresh-context self-check is asserted true. FAIL names the missing/ambiguous criterion or the horizontal phase.
stepSet.conditional: Always runs (the only fully unconditional step). Phase-spec detail is folded in here for small phases; a separate phase-spec sub-pass is emitted inside Step 2 ONLY for phases flagged risk=critical or L+ size.

stepSet.step: 2. Slice + Risk-Route (per phase)
stepSet.objective: Cut the phase into thin AND complete vertical slices, assign each a RISK TIER and SIZE that the rest of the pipeline consumes, and decide each slice's path (fast vs full). The first slice of the first phase must be a tracer bullet piercing every layer for the thinnest happy path with everything else stubbed.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Read the spec + phase. Produce the smallest set of slices where each does exactly ONE observable behavior (statable in one sentence, NO 'and') AND leaves the app runnable with that behavior demoable through its real entry point. Forbid any slice whose answer to 'name the one user-observable behavior' is 'none yet, groundwork' — convert genuine unknowns to a time-boxed spike whose output is a written decision, not committed scaffolding. No slice ships infrastructure no current-or-next slice consumes (rule of two). Apply the sizing table (XS/S/M/L/XL by file count; never label M while accepting L/XL signals). Run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens on every slice/behavior name. Assign each slice a RISK tier by SURFACE DETECTION: CRITICAL if it touches money/auth/data-migration/external-API/destructive ops; TRIVIAL if XS/S and touches none of those and has no user-observable behavior change OR only copy/config/style; else STANDARD. Stop-rule: one slice is correct when splitting would create fake work.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {slices:[{id,name,size,risk:trivial|standard|critical,path:fast|full,observableBehavior,acceptanceCriteriaRefs,surfacesDetected:[money|auth|migration|external|none],isTracerBullet}], phaseSpecProblem|null, assumptions, blockers}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff every slice names exactly one observable behavior with no 'and', leaves the app runnable, has a risk tier + path assigned, and the first slice of phase-01 is a tracer bullet. FAIL names the horizontal/groundwork/over-broad slice or the missing tracer bullet. If phaseSpecProblem is set, route back to Step 1 (upstream).
stepSet.conditional: Always runs per phase. The risk/path assignment here is the lever the whole pipeline pivots on.

stepSet.step: 3. Plan-Build (merged for fast path; plan+canonical-research inline) + Altitude checkpoint
stepSet.objective: For a slice, produce a plan grounded in the EXISTING codebase, fold in canonical evidence only if a trigger surface fired, and (full path) name 2-3 candidate approaches before committing. This step MERGES research+plan and, for fast-path slices, continues straight into build.
stepSet.leanPrompt: READ-BEFORE-WRITE first: locate the real entry points, the existing utilities/validators/conventions this slice must reuse (do NOT create a new helper/adapter/module without grepping for an existing home first), and the likely callers of any shared code you'd touch. ALTITUDE / STEP-BACK (full path only, one paragraph): name 2-3 distinct approaches with a one-line tradeoff each, pick one, say why. SURFACE DETECTION: if the slice touches money/auth/dates/migration/external-API, canonical research is MANDATORY — read current official docs for the installed version, state what Stripe/Amazon/the framework does, verify any new dependency exists and is the genuine popular package (registry + downloads). If no trigger surface, write 'no canonical surface — skipped'. Run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens on any new table/model/type. Attach Stripe-grade guardrails ONLY to detected surfaces (see efficiency moves). Produce a plan: slice boundary, surfaces, approach, test plan anchored to spec input->output examples, verification intent. THEN, fast path only: implement the smallest complete path now, in the same step. If the verified-enough plan cannot be followed without redesigning the slice or fighting the architecture, STOP and emit an obstruction signal (see obstructionLoop) — do not improvise.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {planPath, candidateApproaches:[{name,tradeoff}]|null, canonicalEvidence:[...]|'skipped', existingReuse:[...], adjectiveNounVerdict, splitNeeded, replacementSlices, obstruction:{detected,tier,blastRadius,reversibility}|null, builtInline:bool, changedFiles, readyToVerify, assumptions, blockers}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff plan covers boundary+approach+test-plan, every new table passed the adjective-noun lens, canonical evidence is present for every detected trigger surface (or 'skipped' justified), and existing-code reuse was checked. If obstruction.detected, route per the obstruction loop instead of passing. FAIL names the unsearched duplication, the unjustified new table, or the missing canonical evidence on a money/auth surface.
stepSet.conditional: Always runs per slice. For TRIVIAL/STANDARD (fast path) this is research+plan+build in ONE agent round-trip (saves two round-trips vs the old 5-step). For CRITICAL (full path) build is split out and the step ends at a verified plan.

stepSet.step: 4. Verify-Plan (CONDITIONAL — full path only)
stepSet.objective: Independently verify a CRITICAL slice's plan before any code is written: canonical correctness, adjective-noun, blast radius, that the approach actually satisfies the named acceptance criteria.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Re-read the spec acceptance criteria (the durable artifact, NOT the transcript) and the plan. Confirm: canonical-implementation is satisfied for every money/auth/date/migration/external surface (MANDATORY here, not 'consider'); the adjective-noun verdict holds (no adjective-became-a-table, no over-normalized scalar); the plan reuses existing code rather than duplicating; the chosen approach maps to named acceptance criteria with no off-objective gold-plating; for any one-way-door decision (new persistent schema, new cross-boundary dependency, new public contract) confirm it was escalated, not silently cemented. Emit a per-criterion PASS/FAIL table. Route back to Step 3 (plan) for in-scope fixes, or upstream (Step 1/2) if the plan proves the slice or phase boundary is wrong.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {ready:bool, perCriterion:[{id,verdict}], mustFix:[{id,severity,detail}], canonicalOk, adjectiveNounOk, oneWayDoorEscalated, routeBack, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff ready=true and every acceptance criterion the plan claims to cover is marked satisfiable and canonical/adjective-noun lenses pass. FAIL names the failing criterion or lens; routeBack targets plan (same slice) or upstream step.
stepSet.conditional: CONDITIONAL: runs ONLY for full-path (CRITICAL) slices. CUT entirely for TRIVIAL/STANDARD slices — their plan defects are caught post-build by Step 6 (which already re-reads plan + criteria), removing one agent run + its fix loop from the majority of slices.

stepSet.step: 5. Build (CONDITIONAL — full path only; fast path already built in Step 3)
stepSet.objective: Implement a CRITICAL slice's verified plan with one-hat discipline and surface-triggered guardrails, keeping every change traceable to a named acceptance criterion.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Treat the verified plan as the build handoff; if it does not say Ready, route back and do not edit. Read the named files + nearby code first. TWO HATS: this is a behavior commit — change zero structure (no moves/renames); a needed refactor is a SEPARATE behavior-preserving commit with tests green before and after. Attach the surface-specific guardrails the plan flagged: money -> integer minor units + required currency field + largest-remainder split (ban float/parseFloat); foreign-state mutation -> accept Idempotency-Key, commit local rows in a txn BEFORE the external call, pass a derived key downstream, replay cached response on repeat; destructive migration -> expand (additive nullable) then dual-write+backfill then contract (separate deploys); trust boundary -> parse raw input into a typed/validated object on the first lines, reject with a structured machine-readable error+status, let the type carry inward; consumed contract -> additive only, version/deprecate breaking changes. Record ARCH-DEBT(slice-id) markers in code AND a one-paragraph ADR for any interim shortcut (recorded-in-chat does not count). Run focused checks, not the full suite. If the plan cannot be followed without redesign or the architecture fights you, STOP and emit the obstruction signal.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {buildPath, built:bool, changedFiles, archDebtMarkers:[...], guardrailsApplied:[...], obstruction|null, routeBack, assumptions, blockers}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff built=true, every change maps to a named acceptance criterion (off-objective work is a stall signal, not just inefficiency), the two-hats invariant holds (no structure change in this commit), and every detected surface has its guardrail applied. FAIL or route back otherwise.
stepSet.conditional: CONDITIONAL: runs as a SEPARATE step only for full-path (CRITICAL) slices. For fast-path slices the build already happened inline in Step 3; this step is skipped.

stepSet.step: 6. Verify-Build (honest ladder, risk-scaled)
stepSet.objective: Verify the slice does its ONE observable behavior through the real entry point with real data, scaled to risk tier, and that it composes with prior slices in the phase. The single mandatory gate on every slice.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Re-read the durable acceptance criteria (NOT the transcript) and emit a per-criterion PASS/FAIL table. Climb the verification ladder to the rung the risk tier requires: R0 compile/lint; R1 focused tests pass AND independently diff the test files for tautology/tampering (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded echoes) — anchor every assertion to a spec input->output example, because the implementing agent does not have sole authority over the tests that gate it; R2 exercise the one behavior through its REAL entry path with REAL data and observe output; R3 (UI) actual browser/Playwright run + SCREENSHOT — curl/DOM/static checks are NOT browser verification, report 'code-verified' (never 'verified') with blocked check + residual risk if the browser path is blocked. CRITICAL slices additionally run the MANDATORY canonical + security/state-integrity lenses and a NEGATIVE probe (exercise the spec-named boundary/zero/empty/duplicate input and confirm spec-correct behavior — this catches confidently-wrong one-shot errors that repetition detectors miss). LIGHTWEIGHT COMPOSE CHECK: confirm this slice still composes with prior slices in the phase (shared-surface contracts unbroken) so a mismatch surfaces now, not at phase end. For destructive/external/non-deterministic behavior that cannot be safely demoed, degrade DoD explicitly to a sandbox/dry-run transcript OR an EXTERNAL-VERIFY blocker — never fabricate a green proxy.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {verified:bool, ladderRungReached:R0|R1|R2|R3, perCriterion:[{id,verdict}], testTamperingFound, negativeProbeResult|null, browserEvidence:'verified'|'code-verified'|null, composesWithPriorSlices:bool, mustFix:[{id,severity,detail}], fixNeeded, routeBack, externalVerifyBlockers, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff verified=true, every acceptance criterion is PASS, the required ladder rung for the risk tier was actually reached (TRIVIAL: R1; STANDARD: R2/R3; CRITICAL: R2/R3 + negative probe + mandatory lenses), no test tampering found, and it composes with prior slices. FAIL names the failing criterion; fixNeeded routes to the fix loop (same verifier), routeBack escalates upstream if the plan/phase/spec is wrong.
stepSet.conditional: Always runs per slice — the one universal gate. Fix loop re-runs THIS verifier (bounded by maxFixAttempts); for a single in-place fix the repair is appended to build.md rather than spawning a numbered fix file + fresh agent.

stepSet.step: 7. Verify-Phase / Verify-Initiative (MERGED for single-phase)
stepSet.objective: Confirm the phase's slices integrate into the phase outcome, then (or simultaneously, for a single-phase initiative) confirm the whole initiative satisfies the main spec's acceptance criteria with one final behavior/screenshot pass.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Read the durable acceptance criteria and every slice's build-verification. Verify cross-slice integration: the flows spanning slices work end-to-end through real entry points; shared contracts hold; no half-built feature is live in a broken state (always-runnable is not the same as safe-to-ship — name any partial-feature state or feature-flag/kill-switch that must gate the rollout). Run the adjective-noun lens once more on the assembled domain model and the browser standard once at phase scope (flows spanning slices) — one screenshot pass for the final state if UI. Emit a per-criterion PASS/FAIL table for the whole spec. If an UPSTREAM structural decision proved wrong (the data model can't support a later phase, two phases share a contract that doesn't exist), route back FIRST-CLASS to Step 1 or 2 — this is the escape the old engine logged as 'not auto-handled'.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {verified:bool, perCriterion:[{id,verdict}], crossSliceIntegrationOk, rolloutSafety:{partialStates,killSwitchNeeded}, browserEvidence, mustFix, routeBack:{upstreamStep|null}, ready, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff every main-spec acceptance criterion is PASS, cross-slice/cross-phase integration is observed working, and rollout safety is asserted (no broken half-feature live without a flag). FAIL names the failing criterion; an upstream routeBack re-enters Step 1/2 within budget.
stepSet.conditional: For a SINGLE-PHASE initiative, verify-phase and verify-initiative COLLAPSE into one pass (run the cross-phase/readiness lenses as a delta), saving an entire final verify+fix round. For multi-phase, verify-phase runs per phase and verify-initiative runs once at the end.

obstructionLoop.detection: Detected at Step 3 (plan) or Step 5 (build) by SPECIFIC signals the agent must report as a structured field, NOT self-assessed prose: (a) the verified plan cannot be implemented without changing a file OUTSIDE the slice's named footprint; (b) the change requires touching a SHARED foundation (shared schema, base class, cross-module contract, public API consumed elsewhere); (c) a needed behavior change forces a structural change in the same edit (two-hats violation that cannot be cleanly separated within the footprint); (d) the same acceptance criterion cannot be satisfied because an UPSTREAM artifact (spec shape, phase boundary, a contract another phase assumes) is wrong or missing. PLUS loop-owned mechanical triggers the agent cannot rationalize away: the harness fingerprints (tool + target-region + hash-of-resulting-error); on 2 same-target edits OR 3 turns with no acceptance criterion moving FAIL->PASS OR 2 failed self-corrections, it fires a HARD INTERRUPT that injects this obstruction-routing step. Because a capable agent can perturb the target to dodge a pure fingerprint, the trigger ALSO fires on any edit that reaches outside the slice footprint or modifies a file flagged shared — a footprint/shared-surface tripwire that is harder to game than an error-hash window. The agent never decides whether reassessment happens; it only fills in the blast-radius/reversibility fields.
obstructionLoop.decisionTree: Route STRICTLY by BLAST-RADIUS x REVERSIBILITY (no architectural taste required): TIER 1 — change is IN the slice's existing file footprint AND behavior-preserving -> do a preparatory refactor as a SEPARATE refactor commit (tests green before and after, zero behavior change), then resume the build. Bounded to files the slice already touches. TIER 2 — change needs a NEW seam but is REVERSIBLE (a two-way door: a facade/adapter/interface you can later replace without data loss or breaking consumers) -> insert an ENABLING SLICE behind the facade (thin, demoable, leaves app runnable), build against the facade, continue. TIER 3 — the change is IRREVERSIBLE (a one-way door: new persistent schema, destructive migration, double-charge-class money path, a competing hard-to-reverse design, or a SHARED-FOUNDATION/UPSTREAM decision: spec shape / phase boundary / shared contract is wrong) -> ESCALATE THE DECISION, not the build: write a <=1-page ADR (obstruction, 2-3 candidates with one-line tradeoffs, reversibility classification, recommendation, reversible interim), proceed on the REVERSIBLE INTERIM under a grep-able ARCH-DEBT marker, and route back FIRST-CLASS to the owning upstream step (Step 1 main-spec/phases, or Step 2 slicing) which the engine actually RE-ENTERS (re-runs the owning step, then resumes) — closing the 'escalated route-back not auto-handled in v1' gap. The reversibility classifier is the single point of failure, so the default is conservative: if the agent cannot CONFIDENTLY prove a door is two-way (reversible without data loss or consumer breakage), it is treated as Tier 3 (one-way) and escalated. Momentum never overrides this — when in doubt, escalate.
obstructionLoop.actions: Tier 1: emit a behavior-preserving refactor commit (files-moved? assertion-changed? both checkable from the diff), verify tests green, resume. Tier 2: synthesize a new enabling slice (inserted before the current one in the phase slice list, inherits a risk tier, runs the normal pipeline), build current slice against the facade. Tier 3: write the ADR artifact to the initiative dir, drop ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason+ADR-link at the exact code site, build the reversible interim, and emit an UPSTREAM routeBack {command: reopen-upstream, targetStep: 1|2, reason}. The engine re-enters the named upstream step within an obstruction budget (e.g. maxUpstreamRouteBacks=2 per initiative), re-runs it, then resumes the build from the affected slice. On budget exhaustion: record a blocker, leave the reversible interim in place under its ARCH-DEBT marker, and degrade gracefully rather than loop.
obstructionLoop.recordKeeping: Debt is recorded as CODE, twice, never only in chat: (1) a grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason + ADR-link marker at the exact site; (2) a committed <=1-page ADR (obstruction / 2-3 candidates+tradeoffs / reversibility / recommendation / reversible interim) in the initiative dir. Every obstruction event is appended to the run report's routeBacks/blockers accumulator with {from, tier, blastRadius, reversibility, targetStep, reason} so a hands-off run is auditable. The NEXT slice's Step 3 greps for ARCH-DEBT touching its footprint BEFORE coding, so the interim is visible to the next agent instead of being silently built upon. Recorded-in-chat or recorded-in-PR-body does not count as recorded.

efficiencyMoves: CUT the standalone research-slice step. Canonical research is folded INTO Step 3 plan as 3-6 evidence bullets, and only when surface detection fires a trigger. The unconditional research agent round-trip — the audit's flagged overhead on the majority of slices — is gone; a durable research section survives only for CRITICAL slices the verifier independently re-checks.

MERGE research+plan+build into ONE Step-3 round-trip for the fast path (TRIVIAL/STANDARD). The old pipeline spent 3 separate agent calls (research, plan, build) where a fast-path slice needs one. Build is split back out (Step 5) only for CRITICAL slices.

CUT verify-slice-plan for fast-path slices (make Step 4 conditional on CRITICAL). Plan defects on cheap slices are caught post-build by Step 6, which already re-reads plan + criteria. This removes one full agent run plus its fix loop from every cheap slice — the largest single saving across a multi-slice feature.

DRIVE depth from a single RISK field (trivial/standard/critical) computed ONCE at slice birth (Step 2), consumed everywhere downstream. A typo fix and a payments ledger no longer pay identical toll: TRIVIAL runs Step 2->3->6 (3 gates), CRITICAL runs Step 2->3->4->5->6 (5 gates) with mandatory canonical+security lenses.

COLLAPSE create-phase-spec into Step 1 (spec+phases) for small/tight phases; emit a separate phase-spec sub-pass only for L+ or risk=critical phases. A tightly-scoped single-outcome phase does not need a standalone phase-spec artifact before slicing.
MERGE verify-phase and verify-initiative for single-phase initiatives (Step 7) — run the cross-phase/readiness lenses as a delta in one pass instead of a full second verify+fix round.

FACTOR the browser/user-flow evidence standard into ONE shared 'browser evidence standard' invoked with a scope parameter (slice = this slice's UI / phase = flows spanning slices / initiative = one final screenshot), replacing the ~28 lines triplicated across three verifiers and stopping them from drifting apart.

ATTACH Stripe-grade guardrails ONLY via surface detection. A CSS or copy change triggers zero ceremony (no idempotency, no money-invariant, no migration, no boundary-parsing checks). This is the antidote to guardrail fatigue: the checklist that runs on everything gets skimmed on the one path that matters.
RECORD a single fix as a note appended to build.md rather than spawning a numbered fix-NNN.md + a fresh agent; reserve standalone fix files + dedicated agents for multi-issue or routed-back repairs.

REMOVE all stale state.mjs hints, duplicated PROJECT_LANGUAGE blocks, doubled 'ask one consequential question' lines, and redundant 'reread as if the transcript is gone' restatements — pure prose mass that is inert under the hands-off engine (no user to ask) and confuses sub-agents with a script that does not exist.

BOUND every loop AND add an initiative-level ceiling. Per-loop: maxFixAttempts, maxRouteBacks, maxSplitsPerPhase, maxUpstreamRouteBacks. Global: max total slices and max total agent calls per initiative; on approach, force consolidation or surface a blocker so a runaway decomposition degrades gracefully instead of running forever. Recovery default is revert-to-last-green carrying only a one-paragraph distilled lesson, never append correction #3 in the same context.
approaches.tradeoffs: What I deliberately CUT from the 12-step pipeline and why: (1) the unconditional research-slice agent — folded into plan, triggered only by surface detection; saves a round-trip on most slices, risk is a TRIVIAL slice mislabeled (mitigated: surface detection is mechanical grep, and verify-build still runs canonical lens on CRITICAL). (2) verify-slice-plan as an always-on gate — now CRITICAL-only; saves an agent run + fix loop per cheap slice, risk is a fast-path plan defect reaching code (accepted: it is caught post-build by Step 6 at lower cost than a full plan-verify on every slice, and fast-path slices are by definition low-blast-radius). (3) separate create-phase-spec for small phases — merged into Step 1; risk is under-specifying a phase that turns out complex (mitigated: the L+/risk=critical carve-out still emits a standalone phase-spec). (4) separate verify-initiative for single-phase initiatives — merged into verify-phase. Net: a TRIVIAL slice goes from 5 gated steps to 3; a CRITICAL slice keeps all 5 PLUS gains mandatory canonical/security lenses, the negative probe, and the upstream-escape loop it never had. HONEST RESIDUAL RISKS this design does NOT fully close: (a) the reversibility classifier (Tier 2 vs Tier 3) is still the agent's judgment at the moment of decision — I mitigate with a conservative default (unproven-reversible => treat as one-way => escalate) but cannot eliminate it; an agent biased toward momentum mis-classifying a one-way door is the catastrophic case and remains the single biggest correctness exposure. (b) The acceptance-criteria artifact is LLM-generated in Step 1, so the 'tests encode behavior not intent' failure applies one level up — a wrong criterion silently authorizes the wrong feature and every gate passes green; this design has no fully autonomous fix (it needs the human-ratification path the brief assumes but does not design). (c) Repetition-based stall detection is blind to confident one-shot wrong answers; the CRITICAL negative-probe partially covers this but STANDARD/TRIVIAL slices have no such probe by design (the leanness trade), so a confidently-wrong cheap slice can pass. (d) Multi-agent concurrency, merge-conflict handling, ownership of the durable artifact across parallel agents, and async human-escalation latency (what the reversible interim does while waiting, how a human even SEES the ADR in an autonomous run) are named dependencies I did NOT design here — they are real gaps the brief flags and this single-writer sequential-slice design sidesteps rather than solves. (e) Non-binary/non-deterministic acceptance (UX quality, performance under load, eventual consistency) is forced toward EXTERNAL-VERIFY blockers rather than fabricated green proxies, which is honest but means those properties are not auto-verified — they degrade to human/integration oracles the autonomous run cannot supply.

approaches.approachName: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
approaches.philosophy: Keep every proven Strike gate but make each dramatically leaner and sharper, and tier the depth to the risk already computed. The 12-step linear toll (main-spec -> phases -> phase-spec -> slices -> research -> plan -> verify-plan -> build -> verify-build -> verify-phase -> verify-main-spec) is collapsed to 7 ordered steps, of which 3 are conditional and several merge research+plan or phase-spec+slices for cheap work. Rigor is never cut; verbosity is. Three things the current system buries in prose are promoted to first-class loop-owned gates: (1) ADJECTIVE-NOUN modeling (adjective is an enum/state/scope/permission/config on the existing noun until a new table is ARGUED for with distinct columns AND relationships AND independent lifecycle); (2) CANONICAL/idiomatic research, fired only by a surface-detection trigger so a CSS change pays nothing and a money/auth/dates/crypto change pays mandatory canonical lookup + package-existence check; (3) the OBSTRUCTION loop that routes a fighting-architecture moment by blast-radius and reversibility instead of hacking or spiraling. The non-negotiable backbone: the orchestration LOOP owns every stop/reassess/escalate trigger via mechanical counters; the agent never votes on whether it is stuck or done. Every gate emits a machine-readable verdict naming the failing criterion. Every artifact must pass the fresh-context bar. Verification means the one observable behavior runs through its real entry point with real data, never 'tests pass'. This directly closes the audit's single biggest gap: a first-class route-back to upstream structural steps (phase boundary, main spec) when an upstream decision is wrong, which the v1 engine literally logged as 'not auto-handled'.
approaches.adjectiveNoun: A first-class named lens, the ADJECTIVE-NOUN GATE, runs at three points where durable nouns are born: main-spec/phases naming, slice naming, and plan (before any schema/model/type emit). Default verdict is FIELD-NOT-TABLE. The discriminator is mechanical, not grammatical: CARDINALITY x LIFECYCLE x EXCLUSIVITY. Decision ladder, in order: (1) single-valued + mutually-exclusive + changes-over-the-row's-life (draft->published, active->cancelled) => status COLUMN, CHECK/enum for allowed values; never a second table, never EAV. (2) many-valued + time-assigned (tags, roles a user can hold several of) => join table. (3) a genuinely new real-world thing with its OWN columns AND its OWN relationships AND its OWN independent lifecycle => new table, and the agent must STATE those three in one line or it is not a table. The mechanical falsification test is the UNION SMELL: if answering 'show me all <nouns>' would require UNION across tables (all_posts = drafts UNION published), that alone proves over-splitting -> collapse to one table + discriminator. A *_type column that merely pre-announces a future split is flagged as speculative. The plan step must emit, for every new persistent noun: {noun, decision: column|enum|join-table|new-table, oneLineJustification, unionSmell: yes/no}. verify-plan (full path) and verify-build re-run the lens and FAIL on an un-argued new table. New nouns are reconciled into PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md by whichever step introduces them (closing the audit gap that planners/builders were never required to reconcile terms).
approaches.canonicalResearch: Canonical research is TRIGGER-FIRED, not always-on, killing the audit's 'typo fix and payments ledger pay identical toll' complaint. Step 0 of every slice is a 30-second SURFACE-DETECTION pass (grep + plan reading) that sets boolean flags: touchesMoney, touchesAuthCrypto, touchesDatesTz, touchesMigration, touchesExternalApiOrSdk, touchesTrustBoundary, touchesNewDependency. If ALL flags are false (the common case: copy, CSS, config, internal refactor), canonical research is SKIPPED with a one-line 'no canonical surface' note and research collapses into the plan. The moment ANY money/auth/crypto/dates/accounting/payments flag is true, canonical research becomes MANDATORY (not the current soft 'always consider'): the agent must consult CURRENT official docs / the established library / 'what does Stripe/Amazon do' and record source-backed findings; bespoke implementations of these solved problems are rejected at verify-plan. For touchesNewDependency, the AI-specific SLOPSQUATTING rider fires: verify the package actually exists in the registry and is the genuine popular package (download counts / official source) before importing, because models hallucinate package names attackers pre-publish malware under. Weak evidence is labeled weak. The output is compact: source, finding, planning implication -- never a research transcript or link dump.
approaches.verification: A single HONEST VERIFICATION LADDER, scoped per step, replacing the three near-identical browser sections. Rung 0 (always): tests/build green is NECESSARY but is NEVER reported as 'verified' on its own -- report 'code-verified' at most. Rung 1 (every user-facing or stateful slice, MANDATORY): exercise the ONE observable behavior through its REAL entry point with REAL data and observe the output. curl, DOM inspection, static checks, and code review are explicitly NOT behavior verification. Rung 2 (UI slices): drive the repo-approved browser path (or discover Playwright) and capture a SCREENSHOT; inspect it for loaded-and-styled, no overlap/clip/overflow, values connected to labels, empty/error/success states intact. If blocked by host/auth/env, report 'code-verified' with: what should have run, what blocked it, replacement evidence, residual user-facing risk -- never 'browser-verified'. Rung 3 (only when surface flags fired): money => integer-minor-units + currency-present + parts-sum-exactly; idempotency => replay the key and confirm one side-effect; migration => run expand/backfill/contract against realistic volume; trust boundary => feed malformed input and confirm a typed structured error, not a 500. TEST-TAMPERING GUARD: the implementing agent never has sole authority over the tests that gate it -- every assertion is anchored to a spec-stated input->output example, and the verifier diffs test files for tautology (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded inputs that mirror implementation) rather than just rerunning the suite. CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD (addresses the overfitting-to-looping risk): for any slice with a numeric/threshold/filter parameter (days_held, PID, count, rate), the verifier must show one real run where the value is NON-trivial and the output changes -- defeats the silent days_held=0 / PID=0 class that never loops. Non-binary criteria (UX feel, perf-under-load) are recorded as Live/Human checks with a named proxy, NOT forced into a fake binary PASS.

stepSet.step: S0 Main Spec + Phase Map (merged, one pass)
stepSet.objective: Produce the durable main spec AND the smallest ordered outcome-named phase list in one artifact, so a fresh context can build without re-interviewing the user or reading the chat transcript.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Pull current truth from the refined idea + resolved decisions; preserve accepted decisions, never revive rejected paths. Write the spec with plain boundaries In scope / Out of scope (with why) / Needs user decision / Never. Split success checks into Repo-Verifiable vs Live/Human; invent no vanity metrics. Name engineering pressure points WITHOUT inventing architecture. Run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens on every durable noun (core noun before qualifiers; a qualifier is a field/enum/state/scope/permission/config until argued otherwise) and reconcile terms into PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md. Then break the spec into the SMALLEST ordered set of vertical, outcome-named phases (no schema-first/API-first/UI-later horizontals; a foundation phase only if it retires risk or unlocks the next behavior). Estimate initiative size: total expected slices. HANDS-OFF: convert any one consequential question into a clearly-labeled recorded assumption and continue.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {specPath, phases:[{id,name,outcome,dependsOn,risk:low|standard|high}], languageTermsAdded:[], adjectiveNounDecisions:[{noun,decision,justification}], estimatedSliceCount:int, assumptions:[], openDecisions:[], blockers:[]}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff: spec has all four boundary blocks; every phase is vertical and outcome-named (no horizontal title); every new durable noun has an adjective-noun decision; estimatedSliceCount <= initiative budget (else FAIL: over-decomposition, force consolidation). FRESH-CONTEXT CHECK: re-read as if the transcript is gone -- a planner could proceed without re-interviewing.
stepSet.conditional: Always.

stepSet.step: S1 Phase Spec + Slices (merged for small phases, split for large/high-risk)
stepSet.objective: For one phase, define it precisely enough to slice AND emit the vertical-slice set with acceptance criteria and a per-slice risk tier -- in ONE pass for small phases, two for large.
stepSet.leanPrompt: If the phase is small (few surfaces, one outcome, risk=low/standard) produce phase-spec-and-slices together; if L or risk=high, write the phase spec first, then slice. Preserve the phase boundary; pull only main-spec details that matter here. Slice rule: each slice is ONE observable behavior statable in one sentence with no 'and' AND leaves the app runnable/demoable through a real entry point. FIRST slice is a TRACER BULLET piercing every layer for the thinnest happy path, everything else stubbed -- forbid any slice whose 'one observable behavior' answer is 'none yet, groundwork'. Apply the sizing table (XS=1 file ... XL=8+); never label M while accepting L/XL signals. Set a RISK tier per slice from type+size: low (XS/S non-domain) | standard | high (touches money/auth/data/migration/external-API, or M+). Run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens on slice/behavior names. BOUNDARY-WRONG ESCAPE: if the phase boundary is too broad/small/stale/horizontal or conflicts with the main spec, do NOT invent scope inside slices -- emit boundaryProblem to route back upstream.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {phaseSpecPath?, slices:[{id,name,size,type:Vertical|Non-vertical,risk:low|standard|high,acceptanceCriteria:[binary, test-decidable], surfaceFlags:{money,authCrypto,datesTz,migration,externalApi,trustBoundary,newDep}, isTracerBullet}], boundaryProblem:null|string, languageTermsAdded:[], assumptions:[], blockers:[]}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff: every slice is Vertical or has a written Non-Vertical Justification naming the vertical slice it unblocks; first slice isTracerBullet=true; no slice has an 'and' in its one-behavior sentence; no L/XL unsplit without Why Not Split; every slice has binary acceptance criteria and a risk tier. If boundaryProblem set => ROUTE BACK to S0 (first-class upstream escape).
stepSet.conditional: Always per phase; the spec/slices MERGE for small low/standard phases and SPLIT into two passes for L or high-risk phases.

stepSet.step: S2 Surface-Detect + Plan (with inline evidence; canonical research folded in)
stepSet.objective: Produce a build-ready plan for one slice, with canonical/idiomatic research done ONLY when a real surface triggers it, and the adjective-noun decision recorded for any new persistent noun.
stepSet.leanPrompt: FIRST run the 30-second surface-detection pass (grep + read the slice) and set the surface flags. READ-BEFORE-WRITE: locate the real entry points, existing conventions, and existing utilities/helpers that already cover the need -- do NOT plan a new utility/adapter/shared module without checking the repo has no home (anti-duplication). If ALL surface flags are false: skip standalone research, write a lean plan with a one-line 'no canonical surface' note. If ANY money/auth/crypto/dates/accounting/external-API flag is true: canonical research is MANDATORY -- consult current official docs and the established library/provider ('what does Stripe/Amazon do'), record source-backed findings, and for any NEW dependency verify it exists and is the genuine popular package (registry + download count) to defeat slopsquatting. ADJECTIVE-NOUN GATE before any schema/model/type: emit each new persistent noun's decision (column|enum|join-table|new-table) with one-line justification and the UNION-smell answer; default field-not-table. Name likely files/surfaces, edge cases, a FOCUSED test plan anchored to spec input->output examples (no default full-suite), and the rollback note. SPLIT VALVE: if the plan reveals L/XL signals or multiple behaviors, write a split recommendation instead of a bloated plan. HANDS-OFF: record assumptions, don't ask.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {planPath, surfaceFlags:{...}, canonicalResearch:{needed:bool, findings:[{source,finding,implication}], newDeps:[{name,exists:bool,popular:bool}]}|skippedReason, adjectiveNounDecisions:[{noun,decision,justification,unionSmell}], reusedExisting:[paths], newSharedCode:[{path,home}], testPlan:[{assertion,specExample}], splitNeeded:bool, replacementSlices:[], readyToVerify:bool, assumptions:[], blockers:[]}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff: plan names files/surfaces/edge-cases/focused-tests; every new persistent noun has a field-not-table-defaulted decision with no un-argued new table and no UNION smell; if any money/auth/crypto/dates surface flag is true then canonicalResearch.needed=true and findings are source-backed (else FAIL); every new dependency verified to exist+popular; no new utility duplicates an existing repo home. splitNeeded => SPLIT (re-slice).
stepSet.conditional: Always per slice, but research is INTERNALLY conditional on surface flags (merged into plan when no surface fires).

stepSet.step: S3 Verify Plan (CONDITIONAL gate -- full path only)
stepSet.objective: Catch plan defects before code ONLY for slices where a post-build catch would be expensive: M+ size or any domain/security/data/migration/external-API surface.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Run ONLY for high-risk/M+ slices; SKIPPED for low/standard XS/S non-domain slices (verify-build re-reads the plan and catches those cheaper). Re-run the ADJECTIVE-NOUN lens (FAIL an un-argued new table or a UNION smell). If any surface flag fired, run canonical-implementation as a MANDATORY lens (not 'consider') and the matching Stripe-grade lens (state-data-integrity / security-privacy / integration-risk). Confirm acceptance criteria are binary and test-decidable and the test plan's assertions are anchored to spec examples (not to whatever the code will return). Confirm no new utility duplicates an existing home. Emit a machine-readable verdict naming the failing criterion. ROUTE-BACK, not hack: if the plan needs an upstream decision (phase boundary or spec shape wrong), route back to S1 or S0.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {verdict:PASS|FAIL, failingCriterion:null|string, lensesRun:[], adjectiveNounRecheck:ok|fail, canonicalMandatoryMet:bool|na, mustFix:[{id,severity,detail}], routeBack:{needed,target:S0|S1|S2,reason}, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff no Must-Fix and adjective-noun recheck ok and (if surface fired) canonical lens satisfied. FAIL with fixNeeded => fix loop (loop-owned, bounded). routeBack => upstream re-entry.
stepSet.conditional: CONDITIONAL: runs only for M+ or money/auth/data/migration/external-API slices; SKIPPED otherwise.

stepSet.step: S4 Build (one slice, one hat, obstruction-aware)
stepSet.objective: Implement exactly the planned slice scope with the smallest complete path, wearing ONE hat (behavior xor refactor), and detect-then-route architectural obstruction instead of hacking or spiraling.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Treat the plan as the build handoff. TWO HATS: a behavior change moves/renames no files; a preparatory refactor changes zero behavior with tests green before and after -- never mix in one commit. Implement only the slice scope; do NOT broaden to cleanup or future work (off-objective work is a stall signal, not just inefficiency). Attach Stripe-grade guardrails ONLY where a surface flag fired: idempotency key + local-commit-before-external-call on foreign-state mutations; integer-minor-units + currency + largest-remainder on money; expand/backfill/contract on destructive migrations; parse-at-boundary + typed errors on trust boundaries; established library on auth/crypto/dates. OBSTRUCTION LOOP (see obstructionLoop): when the architecture fights the change, STOP and route by blast-radius+reversibility -- do not improvise around a bad plan. Record ARCH-DEBT(slice-id) markers at the exact site for any interim. Do NOT re-evaluate slice shape (owned upstream). Run focused checks. The LOOP, not you, decides if you are stuck.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {buildPath, built:bool, hat:behavior|refactor, changedFiles:[], guardrailsApplied:[], archDebtMarkers:[{site,reason}], obstruction:null|{tier:1|2|3,action,recorded}, routeBack:{needed,target,reason}, assumptions:[], blockers:[]}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff built=true and hat is single (diff shows behavior xor structure, not both) and every fired surface flag has its guardrail applied. obstruction.tier set => route per the obstruction decision tree. built=false => route back to S2/S3.
stepSet.conditional: Always per slice.

stepSet.step: S5 Verify Build (honest ladder, risk-scoped lenses)
stepSet.objective: Confirm the ONE observable behavior actually works through its real entry point with real data, with lens depth scaled to the slice's risk tier -- never report green tests as 'verified'.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Run the HONEST VERIFICATION LADDER scoped to this slice (see verification). Rung 0 green build is necessary, never sufficient. Rung 1: trigger the one behavior through its REAL entry path with REAL data and observe output -- curl/DOM/static checks are NOT behavior verification (report 'code-verified' if truly blocked). Rung 2 for UI: drive the browser, capture and inspect a SCREENSHOT. Rung 3 only where surface flags fired: money sums exactly / idempotency replay = one side-effect / migration on realistic volume / malformed input => typed error. TEST-TAMPERING GUARD: diff test files for tautology and mocks-returning-the-asserted-value; confirm assertions trace to spec examples; the builder did not have sole authority over these tests. CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD: for any threshold/count/filter param, show one real run with a non-trivial value where output changes. Lens set scales with risk: low slices run a trimmed set (functionality + spec-coverage); high slices add canonical-implementation, security-privacy, state-data-integrity, edge-cases as MANDATORY. INCREMENTAL INTEGRATION: lightweight 'does this still compose with prior slices in this phase' check so a contract break surfaces now, not at phase end. Emit verdict naming the failing criterion.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {verdict:PASS|FAIL, failingCriterion:null|string, behaviorVerified:browser|behavior|code-verified, screenshotPath?, rung3Checks:[], testTamperingClean:bool, confidentWrongCheck:passed|na, composesWithPriorSlices:bool, mustFix:[{id,severity,detail}], liveHumanChecks:[], routeBack:{needed,target,reason}, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff the one behavior is exercised through its real entry point (or honestly downgraded to code-verified with residual risk listed) AND tests are non-tautological AND (UI) a screenshot was inspected AND composes with prior slices AND all fired-surface rung-3 checks pass. FAIL+fixNeeded => bounded fix loop. routeBack => upstream.
stepSet.conditional: Always per slice; lens depth and rung-3 are conditional on risk tier and surface flags.

stepSet.step: S6 Verify Phase + Final (merged when single-phase)
stepSet.objective: Confirm slices compose into the phase outcome and, on the last phase, that phases compose into the main spec -- collapsing the two near-identical passes into one when there is a single phase.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Verify completed slice evidence against the phase spec: every required slice buildVerified=yes, cross-slice flows/contracts intact, adjective-noun lens applied across slices (no two-table draft/published split snuck in), implementation discipline (no cross-slice duplicated utility, shared-surface impact handled). Browser/user-flow check ONLY for flows spanning slices or where slice evidence was thin/blocked. If this is the LAST phase OR the initiative is single-phase, ALSO run the final delta inline: main-spec coverage, cross-phase integration, readiness-risk (are accepted-scope defects hidden as follow-up?), and ONE final screenshot pass for UI -- instead of a whole second verify-main-spec round. SHIP-SAFETY (closes the rollback gap): confirm no half-built feature is live without a flag/kill-switch and any expand/contract migration is backout-safe. Emit verdict naming the failing criterion. UPSTREAM ROUTE-BACK is first-class here: if a phase boundary or the main-spec shape is proven wrong, route back to S1/S0 and the engine RE-ENTERS that step (not 'logged as unhandled').
stepSet.structuredOutput: {verdict:PASS|FAIL, scope:phase|phase+final, slicesVerified:bool, crossSliceOk:bool, adjectiveNounAcrossSlices:ok|fail, finalDelta:{mainSpecCovered,crossPhaseOk,readinessRisk}|na, shipSafe:bool, screenshotPath?, mustFix:[], routeBack:{needed,target:S0|S1,reason}, finalReceipt:{shipped,runUse,next}|na, summary}
stepSet.gate: PASS iff all required slices verified AND cross-slice composes AND (last/single phase) main-spec covered AND shipSafe. routeBack to S0/S1 is honored by the engine (re-run owning step, then resume) under the route-back budget.
stepSet.conditional: Phase verify is always; the FINAL delta MERGES in only on the last/single phase instead of a separate 7th step.

obstructionLoop.detection: Loop-owned signals, NOT agent self-judgment. The harness fires a hard interrupt that injects a fixed reassess step when ANY mechanical counter trips: (a) 2 edits to the SAME target file/region with the resulting error-output hash UNCHANGED (repetition without state change); (b) 3 consecutive build turns with no acceptance criterion moving FAIL->PASS; (c) 2 failed self-corrections on one objective; (d) the build agent emits obstruction=true or proposes a change reaching OUTSIDE the slice's planned file footprint or touching a shared foundation (the refactor-spiral tripwire); (e) the diff shows both moved/renamed files AND changed assertions (two-hats violation). To blunt the gameable-fingerprint risk, the counter also tracks criterion-movement (turns since last FAIL->PASS) which a target-perturbing agent cannot dodge, and footprint-escape, which is checked from the diff not the agent's narration.
obstructionLoop.decisionTree: On interrupt, route by BLAST-RADIUS x REVERSIBILITY (deterministic, needs no architectural taste): TIER 1 = change is in-footprint AND behavior-preserving (the slice's own files, tests green before/after) => allow a PREPARATORY REFACTOR as a separate refactor-hat commit, then resume the behavior commit. TIER 2 = needs a NEW seam but it is REVERSIBLE (two-way door) => insert an ENABLING SLICE behind a facade/interface (a new vertical slice prepended to the list), build against the seam, defer the real implementation. TIER 3 = IRREVERSIBLE (one-way door: new persistent schema, new external/cross-boundary dependency, new public contract, or competing hard-to-reverse design) OR the obstruction proves an UPSTREAM decision wrong (phase boundary / main-spec shape / shared contract) => ESCALATE THE DECISION, not the build: write a <=1-page ADR, proceed on a reversible interim under an ARCH-DEBT marker, and route back to the owning upstream step (S0/S1) which the engine RE-ENTERS. The reversibility classifier is the single point of failure, so the default is conservative: if the agent cannot prove a change is a two-way door, it is treated as Tier 3 (escalate), never silently cemented -- this resolves the 'keep moving vs stop on irreversible' contradiction in favor of safety.
obstructionLoop.actions: Tier 1: refactor-hat commit (no behavior change, tests green both sides), then resume. Never let a Tier-1 refactor reach outside the slice footprint -- the moment it wants to, it becomes Tier 2. Tier 2: prepend one enabling vertical slice (facade + thinnest real path), build behind the seam, continue. Tier 3: write ADR {obstruction, 2-3 candidate approaches each with a one-line tradeoff, reversibility classification, recommendation, reversible interim}; drop a grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason + ADR-link marker at the exact site; route back to the owning upstream step under the route-back budget; the build proceeds on the reversible interim so the run is never fully blocked while the decision is async-ratified. REVERT-AND-RESET primitive (anti-sunk-cost): after 2 failed self-corrections, do NOT append a third instruction -- revert to last green and restart carrying ONLY a one-paragraph distilled lesson, or delegate to a sub-agent whose failed-attempt context is discarded on return. Accumulated failed context is itself the cause of continued failure.
obstructionLoop.recordKeeping: Debt is recorded as CODE, twice, never only in chat (chat does not count): (1) a grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason + ADR-link marker at the exact site; (2) a committed <=1-page ADR. Every route-back and ADR is absorbed into the engine's structured report (routeBacks[], blockers[], assumptions[]) so a hands-off run is auditable and an async human can SEE and ratify the escalation. The NEXT slice's S2 surface-detect step greps for ARCH-DEBT touching its footprint before planning, so the next agent builds on the recorded decision, not the hack. All loops are bounded: maxRouteBacks, maxSplitsPerPhase, maxFixAttempts per loop PLUS an initiative-level ceiling (max total slices / agent calls); on exhaustion the run records a blocker and degrades gracefully (revert-to-last-green carrying the lesson) rather than looping forever.

efficiencyMoves: Collapse 12 steps to 7: merge main-spec+phases (S0), merge phase-spec+slices for small phases (S1), fold research INTO plan via surface-detection (S2), make verify-plan CONDITIONAL (S3), merge verify-phase+verify-main-spec when single-phase (S6).

Fast path vs full path off the risk tier ALREADY computed at slicing: XS/S low-risk non-domain slices run S2(plan-with-inline-evidence)+S4(build)+S5(verify-build) = 3 steps, SKIPPING the standalone S3 verify-plan gate and its fix loop. M+ / money / auth / data / migration / external-API slices run the full S2->S3->S4->S5 path with canonical + security lenses MANDATORY. A typo fix and a payments ledger no longer pay identical toll.

Surface-detection gates canonical research and Stripe-grade guardrails so the happy path (copy/CSS/config/internal refactor) pays ZERO domain ceremony; heavy rigor attaches only where money/mutation/migration/trust-boundary flags fire -- preventing the guardrail-fatigue / ignored-checklist failure the research itself names.

Trivial single-issue fixes append a note to the existing build.md/plan.md instead of spawning a numbered fix-NNN.md + a fresh agent; reserve standalone fix files for multi-issue or routed-back repairs -- removes an agent round-trip on cheap repairs.
