Factor the three triplicated Browser/User-Flow sections and the repeated Route-Back boilerplate into ONE shared standard invoked with a scope parameter; the engine schemas already enforce the null-filling so the skills stop re-teaching it -- cuts prompt mass and drift.

Delete stale ceremony: the dead state.mjs helper-command lines (the JS engine replaced the state machine), the duplicated PROJECT_LANGUAGE block, the 'alert the user' clause (dead in hands-off -> reworded to 'record as assumption/blocker'), and verify-main-spec's route-back enum trimmed to checks the engine actually honors (drop ideaRefined/decisionsResolved).
Initiative-level budget (max total slices / agent calls) added so over-decomposition degrades gracefully instead of exploding unbounded across phases x slices x 5 steps x fix loops.
On-disk slices/ directory is the source of truth: on phase entry reconcile the in-memory slice list against disk so resume-after-a-split is deterministic (closes the renumbering/idempotency gap).
approaches.tradeoffs: ["MODALITY: surface-detection flags and the field-not-table heuristic are SQL/web-app shaped. For non-SQL modalities (Kafka topic schema, dbt model, mobile feature-flag, IaC, LLM-app prompt/RAG) the adjective-noun gate degrades to its general form (is this qualifier a state/config on an existing entity?) and Stripe-grade money/migration guardrails simply do not fire (their flags stay false). The cost: these modalities get the lean skeleton but not bespoke guardrails -- an accepted v1 limitation, surfaced explicitly rather than mis-routed.","NON-BINARY ACCEPTANCE: forcing every criterion into a binary test-decidable token risks fabricating brittle proxies for genuinely non-deterministic/UX/perf properties. Mitigation: such criteria are recorded as Live/Human checks with a NAMED proxy and residual risk, NOT a fake PASS -- the gate stays honest at the cost of not auto-closing them.","REVERSIBILITY CLASSIFIER is the single point of failure (agent must tell one-way from two-way doors). Mitigation: conservative default -- unproven two-way => treated as Tier 3 escalate. This trades some momentum (occasionally escalating a truly reversible change) for never silently cementing an irreversible one, which is the catastrophic case.","GAMEABLE FINGERPRINTS: a capable sunk-cost agent can perturb the target/reword the command to dodge a tool+target+error-hash window. Mitigation: the loop ALSO counts criterion-movement (turns since last FAIL->PASS) and footprint-escape from the DIFF, which target-perturbation cannot evade; and the revert-and-reset primitive discards the gaming context entirely.","CONFIDENT one-shot wrongness (days_held=0, PID=0) is invisible to repetition-based detectors. Mitigation: the verify-build CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD forces a real run with a non-trivial parameter value where output changes -- but this is a partial defense; the higher-severity silent class remains the workflow's hardest residual risk and is flagged, not claimed solved.","HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP latency: Tier-3 ADR ratification assumes a responsive human oracle the autonomous run may not have. Mitigation: the run NEVER blocks on the human -- it proceeds on the reversible interim under ARCH-DEBT and surfaces the ADR in the structured report for async ratification; the interim itself can be wrong, an accepted risk bounded by the route-back budget.","ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA GENESIS: the load-bearing binary checklist is LLM-generated, so the 'tests encode behavior not spec' failure can recur one level up. Mitigation: criteria are written at S0/S1 from the user's resolved decisions BEFORE any code exists, anchored to stated input->output examples, and the verifier (separate authority from the builder) checks assertions trace to them -- but a wrong/incomplete spec still silently authorizes the wrong feature, the deepest unmitigated dependency.","SOURCE QUALITY: headline efficiency claims (7x, 60-70%) rest on blog/anecdote-grade evidence; the gate structure is adopted on engineering judgment, not settled proof, and should be A/B-measured in practice rather than asserted."]

approaches.approachName: Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine
approaches.philosophy: Center every decision on ALTITUDE + SELF-CORRECTION, but move the act of self-correction OUT of the agent's judgment and INTO the orchestration loop. The agent never votes on whether it is stuck, done, or off-track; mechanical counters owned by the engine fire hard interrupts the agent cannot rationalize away. Around that backbone hang three things, in priority order: (1) the Architectural-Obstruction Loop — the one place a build step can step back and re-route by blast-radius/reversibility instead of hacking forward or spiraling into a 40-file refactor; this is the system's spine and the current engine's single biggest correctness gap (it literally logs 'escalated route-back not auto-handled in v1'). (2) Honest verification — 'verified' means the one observable behavior ran through its real entry point with real data, never 'tests pass' or 'code looks correct.' (3) Modeling + canonical discipline — adjective-noun ('field, not table' by default) and canonical-implementation, attached only where a detection pass finds their trigger surface. The whole thing is risk-tiered: a CSS change and a payments ledger must not pay the same toll, or the agent learns to skip the checklist that guards the money path. Leanness is not a nice-to-have; ceremony-on-everything IS the failure mode the research names. So I CUT the pipeline from 12 fixed steps to a 4-to-7-step variable path: a 3-step fast lane for XS/S non-domain slices and a full lane only for M+/domain/irreversible work, with a first-class upstream route-back that the engine actually re-enters (not just logs).
approaches.canonicalResearch: Canonical/idiomatic research is its own micro-step ONLY when a detection pass flags a trigger surface; otherwise it collapses into planning. The detection pass (cheap, deterministic, run once at slice creation from the slice's file footprint + diff intent) sets a `domainFlags` set: {money, auth, crypto, dates-tz, accounting, payments, external-API, migration, new-dependency}. Rules: (a) If domainFlags is empty AND size is XS/S, NO standalone research — the planner does an inline one-line evidence note ('no external deps, no decision; uses existing X helper') and proceeds. (b) If ANY domainFlag is set, canonical-implementation research is MANDATORY (not 'always consider'): pull the CURRENT official docs and answer 'what does Stripe/Amazon/the standard do for this exact thing' before any code; invented financial/auth/crypto/timezone logic is rejected at the plan gate. (c) Any NEW dependency triggers a slopsquatting check: confirm the package actually exists in the registry and is the genuine popular one (download counts), because models hallucinate plausible names attackers pre-publish malware under. (d) Research output is implications, not a transcript: source -> finding -> planning-implication, three lines each, weak evidence labeled weak. The valve 'in rare cases research is not needed, say why in one sentence' is preserved and generalized — research that doesn't change the outcome is not run.
approaches.verification: Honest verification ladder, climbed only as high as the slice's risk demands; the implementing agent NEVER has sole authority over the tests that gate it. Rungs, lowest to highest: (R0) static/build/lint passes — necessary, never sufficient, never reported as 'verified'. (R1) focused tests pass, AND a separate verifier diffs the test files for tautology (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded inputs, assertions that mirror current output rather than the spec example) — because >79% of agent cheating is test tampering and agent-written tests encode the code's buggy behavior, not the spec. Every assertion must anchor to a spec-stated input->output example. (R2) BEHAVIOR rung (mandatory for any user-facing or stateful slice): trigger the ONE observable behavior through its REAL entry point with REAL data and observe the output. Code review, curl, static checks, and DOM inspection are explicitly NOT this. (R3) BROWSER rung for UI: drive the repo-approved browser path (or discovered Playwright) and capture a screenshot inspected for: loaded+styled (not blank/raw HTML), no layout collapse/overlap/clip, values connected to correct labels, empty/error/success states don't break the main UI, no horizontal overflow or hidden primary actions. When any rung is blocked by host/auth/env, the verdict is 'code-verified' with the residual user-facing risk listed — NEVER 'verified'. For irreversible/external-effect actions that can't be safely demoed (charges, destructive migrations, emails), the behavior rung runs against a sandbox/dry-run/staging entry point or a recorded-fixture replay, and the residual is logged as Accepted Risk with the real-path gap named. The verdict contract stays crisp and machine-readable: Verified yes/no + the named failing criterion — ambiguous prose ('looks mostly good') is the documented cause of retry loops and is banned.
approaches.adjectiveNoun: Field-not-table is a first-class named lens at three altitudes with a mechanical UNION-smell falsification test (see adjectiveNoun field above for full detail).

stepSet.step: 0. Detect + Tier (engine-side, no agent call on the happy path)
stepSet.objective: Compute each slice's risk tier and trigger surfaces ONCE at slice creation, so every downstream step's depth is driven by data already on disk — not recomputed per step.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Folded into create-phase-slices output. For each slice emit: size (XS..XL from the file-count table), type (Vertical/Non-vertical), and domainFlags = any of {money,auth,crypto,dates-tz,accounting,payments,external-API,migration,new-dependency,destructive-op} detected from the slice's named surfaces. Derive tier: FAST if size in {XS,S} AND domainFlags empty AND type Vertical; FULL otherwise. Run the adjective-noun lens on the slice/behavior NAME here and record the column-vs-table call for any adjective-noun sibling.
stepSet.structuredOutput: slices[]: {id,name,size,type,domainFlags[],tier:FAST|FULL,adjNounCall:{noun,decision:column|enum|join|table,unionSmell:bool},dependsOn[]}; phaseSpecProblem|null
stepSet.gate: Every slice has a tier and (if it names an adjective-noun) an adjNounCall. No slice titled with 'and/full/complete/MVP/setup'. No horizontal slice (a slice whose one-behavior answer is 'none yet, groundwork') — convert to a time-boxed spike whose output is a written decision. First slice is a tracer bullet piercing all layers.
stepSet.conditional: Always (replaces standalone slice step; absorbs sizing + detection + naming-lens).

stepSet.step: 1. Plan (+inline evidence, +canonical if flagged)
stepSet.objective: Produce a build-ready plan; for FAST slices this is the ONLY pre-build step (research folded in). Name 2-3 approaches with one-line tradeoffs before committing — the cheap reverse gear LLMs lack.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Read slice stub + phase spec + PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md. STEP-BACK (one paragraph, mandatory, not ceremony): name 2-3 distinct approaches, one-line tradeoff each, pick one, say why. Inspect repo for existing utilities/conventions/entry points BEFORE planning edits — do not invent a helper that exists. Apply adjective-noun lens to any new type/route/schema (default field-not-table). If FULL tier and any domainFlag: do canonical research inline — current official docs, 'what does Stripe/Amazon/the standard do', verify any new dependency exists + is the popular package (download counts). If FAST and no flags: one-line evidence note + proceed. Name likely files, surfaces, edge cases, focused tests anchored to spec input->output examples, and the behavior-verification entry point. Output the smallest complete path, not a coding script.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {planPath, approachesConsidered[], chosenApproach, evidenceNotes[], canonicalChecks[]|null, depVerification[]|null, splitNeeded:bool, replacementSlices[], adjNounCalls[], readyToBuild:bool (FAST) | readyToVerify:bool (FULL)}
stepSet.gate: Plan names files+surfaces+focused tests+behavior entry point without guessing; step-back paragraph present; if domainFlag set, canonicalChecks non-empty and any new dep verified; splitNeeded handled. FAST tier: readyToBuild=true ends pre-build. FULL: readyToVerify=true -> step 2.
stepSet.conditional: Always. Merges research+plan. Research is a separate prior agent call ONLY when FULL tier AND domainFlags includes external-API/payments/auth (where evidence is heavy enough to warrant isolation).

stepSet.step: 2. Verify Plan
stepSet.objective: Catch plan defects (wrong model, missing canonical pattern, oversized slice, duplicated utility) before any code is written — but only when the stakes justify a standalone gate.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Re-read the FIXED slice acceptance criteria (durable artifact, NOT the transcript). Emit a per-criterion PASS/FAIL table. Run lenses scaled to tier: always implementation-plan + adjective-noun. MANDATORY (not 'consider') canonical-implementation + security-privacy + state-data-integrity when domainFlags include money/auth/data/migration/external-API. Confirm: plan uses real repo patterns, no duplicate shared code, new table is argued (3 properties) or it's a column, slice still fits one build loop. If an UPSTREAM decision is wrong (spec shape / phase boundary / shared contract that can't support this), set routeBack to the OWNING upstream step — this is the first-class upstream escape.
stepSet.structuredOutput: VERDICT {ready,fixNeeded,artifactPath,perCriterion[],mustFix[],routeBack{needed,command:reopen-slice-check|reopen-phase-check|reopen-main-spec,target,reason},summary}
stepSet.gate: Ready=true (all criteria PASS, mandatory lenses ran for flagged surfaces) -> build. fixNeeded -> fix loop. routeBack -> engine re-enters the named upstream step (capped by budget).
stepSet.conditional: SKIPPED for FAST tier (verify-build re-reads the plan and catches plan defects post-build). MANDATORY for FULL tier and any domain/security/data/migration/external-API slice.

stepSet.step: 3. Build (with built-in step-back -> Obstruction Loop)
stepSet.objective: Implement exactly one slice's one behavior, smallest complete path, attach Stripe-grade guardrails only to detected surfaces — and step back into the Obstruction Loop the instant the architecture fights, instead of hacking or spiraling.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Treat plan as the handoff. Grep for ARCH-DEBT markers touching this footprint FIRST. Implement only this slice; one hat (behavior commit moves no files; refactor commit changes no assertions, green before+after). Attach guardrails ONLY where the surface-detection flagged them: idempotency-key + local-commit-before-external-call on foreign-state mutations; integer-minor-units + currency + largest-remainder on money; expand/contract on destructive migrations; parse-at-boundary + typed errors on trust boundaries; canonical library (no hand-rolled JWT/crypto/tz/rounding) on auth/crypto/dates. A CSS/copy change attaches NONE. Add focused tests anchored to spec examples (you do NOT have sole authority — a separate verifier audits them). OBSTRUCTION DETECTION: if you cannot implement the planned path without (a) editing files outside the slice footprint, (b) touching a shared foundation, (c) a new persistent schema/contract not in the plan, or (d) choosing between competing hard-to-reverse designs -> STOP, do not improvise. Emit obstruction with blast-radius + reversibility so the engine routes it.
stepSet.structuredOutput: {buildPath,built:bool,changedFiles[],guardrailsAttached[],oneHatOk:bool,obstruction{hit:bool,tier:1|2|3,blastRadius:in-footprint|new-seam|shared-foundation,reversibility:two-way|one-way,candidates[],reason}|null,routeBack,assumptions[],blockers[]}
stepSet.gate: built=true AND oneHatOk AND (guardrails for every flagged surface present) -> verify-build. obstruction.hit -> Obstruction Loop (see loop). built=false w/ routeBack -> engine honors.
stepSet.conditional: Always.

stepSet.step: 4. Verify Build (honest behavior ladder)
stepSet.objective: Prove the ONE observable behavior actually runs through its real entry point with real data — and that tests weren't tampered to pass. The load-bearing anti-hallucination gate.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Re-read fixed acceptance criteria; emit per-criterion PASS/FAIL. Climb the ladder only as high as risk demands: R0 build/lint (never 'verified' alone) -> R1 focused tests pass + a SEPARATE tautology audit of the test diff (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded inputs, assertions mirroring current output not the spec) -> R2 trigger the one behavior through its REAL entry point with REAL data and observe output (mandatory for user-facing/stateful) -> R3 for UI, drive the browser + capture+inspect a screenshot (loaded/styled, no overlap/clip/overflow, values match labels, states don't break UI). curl/DOM/static are NOT browser verification. Irreversible/external actions: verify via sandbox/dry-run/replay, log the real-path gap as Accepted Risk. Lenses scaled to tier (functionality + spec-coverage + edge-cases always; security/state/integration when flagged). Report 'code-verified' (never 'verified') when a rung is blocked, with residual risk.
stepSet.structuredOutput: VERDICT {ready,fixNeeded,perCriterion[],ladderReached:R0|R1|R2|R3,behaviorEntryPoint,evidence,tautologyAudit:pass|issues,screenshotFindings|null,mustFix[],acceptedRisk[],routeBack,summary}
stepSet.gate: ready=true requires per-criterion all PASS AND ladderReached>=R2 for user-facing/stateful (>=R3 or explicit Accepted-Risk for UI) AND tautologyAudit=pass. fixNeeded -> fix. routeBack (incl. upstream) -> engine re-enters.
stepSet.conditional: Always. For FAST tier it also absorbs the skipped plan-verification (re-reads plan).

stepSet.step: 5. Verify Phase (delta, collapses into final for single-phase)
stepSet.objective: Confirm slices compose — cross-slice integration, shared-contract consistency, phase-spec coverage — without re-auditing each slice.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Read phase spec + each slice's build-verification (must be Verified:yes). Run phase-spec-coverage + cross-slice-integration always; adjective-noun lens on the assembled domain model; security/state/integration when those surfaces span slices. Browser only when a user flow SPANS slices or slice evidence was thin/blocked. INCREMENTAL HOOK: this also reconciles the in-memory slice list against the on-disk slices/ dir (source of truth) so resume-after-split is deterministic. If an upstream structural decision proves wrong, routeBack to phase-spec or main-spec.
stepSet.structuredOutput: VERDICT {ready,fixNeeded,sliceStatus[],crossSliceFindings[],adjNounModelOk,routeBack,summary}
stepSet.gate: ready=true (every required slice Verified, phase spec satisfied, slices compose). routeBack -> engine re-enters owning step.
stepSet.conditional: Always per phase, BUT when there is exactly ONE phase it runs the final cross-initiative readiness lenses inline as a delta and the separate Verify-Main-Spec is skipped entirely.

stepSet.step: 6. Verify Main Spec (final gate)
stepSet.objective: Final initiative gate: completed phases satisfy the main spec, compose coherently, and one final UI screenshot pass holds.
stepSet.leanPrompt: Read main spec + each phase verification (Ready:yes). main-spec-coverage + cross-phase-integration + readiness-risk (is any accepted-scope defect hidden as follow-up?). One final browser/screenshot pass for UI initiatives. Emit the Final Receipt (Shipped / Run-Use / Next) so a fresh context can ship it. Route-back enum trimmed to checks the engine actually honors (no ideaRefined/decisionsResolved — those happen in chat, out of engine).
stepSet.structuredOutput: VERDICT {ready,fixNeeded,phaseStatus[],crossPhaseFindings[],screenshotCheck,finalReceipt,routeBack,summary}
stepSet.gate: ready=true -> initiative verified. Else blocker recorded; degrade gracefully.
stepSet.conditional: SKIPPED when single-phase (folded into step 5). MANDATORY for multi-phase.

obstructionLoop.detection: The engine — not the agent's self-judgment — owns detection via TWO channels. CHANNEL A (declared, fast): build-slice emits a structured `obstruction` object whenever the planned path cannot proceed without one of four mechanical triggers: (a) editing a file OUTSIDE the slice's recorded footprint, (b) touching a shared foundation, (c) a new persistent schema/contract/public API not in the plan, or (d) a choice between competing hard-to-reverse designs. These are surface facts (file paths, schema verbs, contract edits) the engine can corroborate against the diff, NOT a feeling. CHANNEL B (mechanical stall counters, ungameable-by-construction): the loop fingerprints {tool + target-region + hash-of-resulting-error} per turn and fires a HARD INTERRUPT on: 2 same-target edits with unchanged error, OR 3 turns with no acceptance criterion moving FAIL->PASS, OR 2 failed self-corrections on one objective, OR per-loop budget exhaustion. Because a capable agent can perturb the target to dodge a single fingerprint, the counters are AND-gated with a coarse 'criteria-moved?' signal: if no criterion flips FAIL->PASS within the window, the interrupt fires regardless of how the target was perturbed — you cannot dodge 'made zero progress.' Channel A catches the architectural case the agent CAN see; Channel B catches the fixated case it can't.
obstructionLoop.decisionTree: On obstruction, route deterministically by BLAST-RADIUS x REVERSIBILITY (no architectural taste required): TIER 1 — in-footprint AND behavior-preserving -> a PREPARATORY REFACTOR, hard-bounded to files the slice already touches, tests green before+after (two-hats: zero behavior change). Resume the build. TIER 2 — reversible NEW seam, two-way door -> insert an ENABLING SLICE behind a facade/interface; the original slice proceeds against the seam. Add the new slice to the phase list (counts against maxSplitsPerPhase). TIER 3 — irreversible (one-way door) OR competing hard-to-reverse design OR shared-foundation/upstream-decision wrong -> ESCALATE THE DECISION, not the build: write a <=1-page ADR {obstruction, 2-3 candidates w/ one-line tradeoffs, reversibility, recommendation, reversible interim}, drop a grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id) marker at the exact site, PROCEED on the reversible interim, and surface the ADR for async ratification. If the wrong decision is UPSTREAM (spec shape, phase boundary, shared contract), the SAME Tier-3 path emits a first-class routeBack to create-main-spec / create-development-phases / create-phase-spec — and the engine ACTUALLY RE-ENTERS that step then resumes (fixing the current engine's 'escalated route-back not auto-handled in v1' gap). The reversibility classifier is the single point of failure, so it is conservative-by-default: ambiguous -> treat as one-way (Tier 3). Anything touching money/migrations/auth/external-effects is one-way by rule, never agent-judged.
obstructionLoop.actions: Tier 1: behavior-preserving refactor inside footprint, then continue build. Tier 2: create facade + enabling slice, register it, build original against seam. Tier 3: write ADR + ARCH-DEBT marker + proceed on reversible interim + (if upstream) routeBack to the owning upstream step which the engine re-enters. On a Channel-B stall interrupt specifically: REVERT-AND-RESET, not continue-and-correct — discard the polluted context, restore last green state, restart the objective carrying ONLY a one-paragraph distilled lesson (or delegate to a sub-agent whose failed-attempt context is dropped on return). Never reach correction #3 in the same context window.
obstructionLoop.recordKeeping: Debt is recorded as CODE, twice, never in chat: (1) a grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id): reason + ADR-link marker at the exact site; (2) a committed <=1-page ADR artifact. The next slice's first build action greps ARCH-DEBT touching its footprint. The engine logs every obstruction to report.routeBacks/report.blockers with {from, tier, blastRadius, reversibility, command, target, reason}, and every loop is bounded: maxRouteBacks, maxSplitsPerPhase, maxFixAttempts, PLUS a new initiative-level ceiling (max total slices / max agent calls). On any budget exhaustion the run records a blocker and DEGRADES GRACEFULLY (revert to last green, surface the blocker) rather than looping forever. Resume re-reconciles in-memory slices against the on-disk slices/ dir so a mid-phase split/route-back is deterministic on replay.

efficiencyMoves: CUT the 12-step fixed pipeline to a variable 4-7 steps. FAST lane (XS/S non-domain vertical slices = the majority) = Plan -> Build -> Verify-Build (3 steps, ONE pre-build agent call). FULL lane = Plan -> Verify-Plan -> Build -> Verify-Build (+ phase/main verify). A typo fix and a payments ledger no longer pay the same toll.

MERGE research into plan. Research becomes an inline evidence note for FAST slices and a separate agent call ONLY for FULL+heavy-domain (external-API/payments/auth). Removes one full agent round-trip on most slices.

MAKE verify-plan conditional. Skip the standalone plan-verification gate for FAST slices — verify-build already re-reads the plan and catches plan defects post-build. Drops one agent run + its fix loop from every cheap slice.

COLLAPSE verify-main-spec into verify-phase when there is exactly ONE phase (run the cross-initiative readiness lenses inline as a delta). Saves an entire final verify+fix round on single-phase initiatives.
FOLD phase-spec into slicing for small, tightly-scoped phases — produce spec-and-slices in one pass; keep a standalone phase-spec only for L/risky phases.
FOLD detection+sizing+naming-lens into slice creation (computed ONCE, consumed everywhere) instead of re-deriving risk at each step.
FACTOR the browser/user-flow standard into ONE shared evidence rung invoked with a scope param (slice/phase/final) instead of three near-identical ~28-line copies that drift.

LEAN debt recording: a single in-place Must-Fix is a note appended to the existing build.md, not a fresh numbered fix-NNN.md + a new agent; reserve standalone fix files for multi-issue or routed-back repairs.

DELETE dead/stale prose: the 'ask the user' clauses (no user in a hands-off run -> 'record as assumption'), the duplicated PROJECT_LANGUAGE block, the stale state.mjs hints (the JS engine replaced state.json), and the redundant 'reread as if the transcript is gone' restatements (keep once per skill in the Quality Bar).

GATE the ceremony itself on risk: surface-detection, canonical research, and the heavy lens set run only when domainFlags fire — so the aggregate guardrail cost is near-zero on the happy path and concentrated on the money/auth/migration surfaces that actually need it.
approaches.tradeoffs: "What I keep vs cut and the costs. KEPT VERBATIM: the fresh-context quality bar (strongest idea in the system), the four plain scope boundaries, the Ready/Verified machine-readable verdict contract, the browser-vs-code-verified honesty gate, the same-verifier fix loop, bounded route-backs, HANDS_OFF (ask->record-assumption), the sizing table, 'avoid horizontal phases', and the canonical-implementation mandate. CUT/COLLAPSED: standalone research for cheap slices, standalone verify-plan for cheap slices, separate phase-spec for small phases, separate verify-main-spec for single-phase, the three duplicated browser sections, fix-NNN ceremony for single fixes, and ~all dead prose. NET STEP COUNT: most slices 12->effectively 3 agent calls; high-stakes slices keep the full depth. RISKS I ACCEPT AND HOW: (1) Skipping verify-plan on FAST slices risks a plan defect reaching code — mitigated because FAST = XS/S non-domain (low blast radius) and verify-build re-reads the plan; the tier is data-driven, not agent-chosen, so it can't be gamed into FAST. (2) The reversibility classifier is the single point of failure (an agent biased to momentum can mislabel a one-way door as two-way) — mitigated by conservative-by-default (ambiguous->Tier 3) and a HARD RULE that money/migrations/auth/external-effects are one-way regardless of agent judgment; this is the one place I refuse to trust agent taste. (3) Mechanical stall fingerprints are perturbable by a sunk-cost agent — mitigated by AND-gating with 'did any criterion flip FAIL->PASS', so 'made zero real progress' fires the interrupt no matter how the target/command/error is reworded. (4) Repetition-based guardrails are BLIND to confident one-shot wrong answers (days_held=0 skips every trade, all tests green) — this is the higher-severity class and my honest gap; the only defense is the R2 behavior rung (real entry point, real data, observe output) + spec-anchored assertions audited by a separate verifier, which catches the silent-wrong case the stall counters never see. (5) Binary PASS/FAIL gates can't express non-deterministic/UX/perf/eventual-consistency acceptance — for those the criterion is stated as a bounded observable (p95 < X on N requests; converges within T) or explicitly carried as Accepted Risk with the real property named, rather than faking a brittle green proxy. ACKNOWLEDGED UNADDRESSED (out of scope but real): non-SQL modalities (Kafka/dbt/mobile/IaC) where adjective-noun and Stripe-money guardrails don't transfer; multi-agent file-ownership/merge mechanics; the human-oracle latency that Tier-3 async ratification silently depends on; and who authors/keeps-correct the acceptance-criteria artifact that three gates hang on (if it's LLM-generated, the 'tests encode behavior not spec' failure recurs one level up). These are the next design iteration."

ranking: Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine
Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates

scores.approach: Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine
scores.note: Strongest obstruction handling of the three: the dual-channel detection (Channel A declared/architectural that the engine corroborates against the diff; Channel B mechanical stall counters AND-gated with 'did any criterion flip FAIL->PASS') is the single most rigorous and least-gameable formulation in the set. It explicitly answers the 'capable agent perturbs the target to dodge the fingerprint' attack with a progress signal that cannot be reworded around, and pairs it with REVERT-AND-RESET (discard polluted context, restore last green, restart with a one-paragraph lesson) so the loop never reaches correction #3 in-context. Step 0 'Detect+Tier' is computed engine-side with NO agent call on the happy path -- the leanest tier assignment of the three, and risk is data-driven so it 'can't be gamed into FAST.' Hard rule that money/migrations/auth/external-effects are one-way regardless of agent judgment removes the reversibility classifier as a trust point exactly where it matters most -- a sharper safety stance than D1/D2's purely conservative-default. Honest verification ladder is fully spelled out with R2 behavior rung as the named defense against the confident-one-shot-wrong class. The 2-tier (FAST/FULL) model is slightly coarser than D1/D2's 3-tier, which is the main cost: a 'standard' slice that is M-size but non-domain falls into FULL and pays verify-plan it might not need. Tradeoffs section is the most surgically honest (names the days_held=0 silent-wrong class as 'my honest gap' and the R2 rung as the only defense). Most lean: most slices collapse to 3 agent calls.

scores.approach: Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
scores.note: The most aggressive merge: research+plan+build collapse into ONE Step-3 round-trip for fast path (TRIVIAL/STANDARD), build split back out only for CRITICAL. This is the leanest pre-build path of the three on paper and the boldest cost cut. Excellent 3-tier granularity (TRIVIAL/STANDARD/CRITICAL) gives finer routing than D3's 2-tier -- a true TRIVIAL copy/config slice stops at R0+R1 with no behavior demo, which D3 would still push to R2. Obstruction loop is essentially as strong as D3's: footprint/shared-surface tripwire 'harder to game than an error-hash window,' conservative reversibility default, first-class upstream re-entry with maxUpstreamRouteBacks budget. The CRITICAL-only NEGATIVE/adversarial probe (exercise the boundary/zero/empty/duplicate input) is a concrete, named defense against confidently-wrong errors that is slightly more actionable than D3's general R2 framing. Where it loses to D3: (1) merging build INTO plan on the fast path mixes the planning hat and the building hat in one agent context -- the design itself preaches one-hat discipline, and a single round-trip that both plans and builds erodes the independent-plan checkpoint and the separation that makes obstruction-at-plan-time detectable before code exists. (2) Its stall detection leans on the error-hash fingerprint + footprint tripwire but does NOT as cleanly foreground the AND-gated 'no criterion moved FAIL->PASS' progress signal that makes D3 ungameable; it mentions criterion-stalling but D3 makes it the load-bearing dodge-proof gate. Tradeoffs section is the most exhaustive and honest of all three (residuals a-e). Step labeling is muddled (titled '6-Step' but emits 7 steps).

scores.approach: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
scores.note: Solid, rigorous, and faithful -- but the least lean of the three because it KEEPS build as an always-separate step (S4) for every slice and never merges build into plan even on the cheap path. Its fast path is S2(plan)+S4(build)+S5(verify) = 3 steps, same count as the others, but it forgoes the bolder research+plan+build merge D1 attempts and the engine-side no-agent-call tier-detection D3 attempts, so per-slice agent-call cost is marginally higher. Has two genuinely best-in-class operational details the others underweight: (1) explicit PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md reconciliation BY whichever step introduces a new noun (closing the audit gap that planners/builders were never required to reconcile terms) -- the most concrete fix for term drift; (2) on-disk slices/ directory as source of truth with reconcile-against-disk on phase entry so resume-after-split is deterministic (the renumbering/idempotency gap) -- the only design that treats this as a first-class step concern (D3 only mentions it inside the loop). Obstruction loop is strong and near-identical in structure to D1/D2 (blast-radius x reversibility, conservative default, first-class upstream re-entry) but its stall detection is described as OR-gated counters with criterion-movement added 'which a target-perturbing agent cannot dodge' -- correct, but stated less crisply than D3's explicit AND-gate. The UNION-SMELL and *_type speculative-column flag for adjective-noun is the most fully specified modeling lens. Modality tradeoff (Kafka/dbt/mobile/IaC degradation of the adjective-noun gate) is the most explicit of the three. Overall: the most engineering-operationally complete but pays for it with slightly more ceremony and the least daring leanness.

bestElementsToGraft: SC-Strike's dual-channel obstruction detection: Channel A = declared/architectural obstruction the engine corroborates against the actual diff (file paths outside footprint, new schema verbs, contract edits); Channel B = mechanical stall counters AND-GATED with a coarse 'did any acceptance criterion flip FAIL->PASS in the window' progress signal, so a sunk-cost agent that perturbs the target/command/error to dodge a fingerprint still trips on 'made zero real progress.' This is the single least-gameable stall detector in the set and should be the canonical formulation.

SC-Strike's REVERT-AND-RESET primitive on a Channel-B stall: discard the polluted context, restore last-green state, and restart the objective carrying ONLY a one-paragraph distilled lesson (or delegate to a sub-agent whose failed-attempt context is dropped on return) -- never reach correction #3 in the same context window, because accumulated failed context is itself the cause of continued failure.

SC-Strike's HARD RULE that money/migrations/auth/external-effects are classified one-way (Tier 3, escalate) BY RULE regardless of agent judgment -- removing the reversibility classifier (the system's named single point of failure) from agent taste exactly on the surfaces where a misclassification is catastrophic, rather than relying only on the conservative-default heuristic.

SC-Strike's engine-side Step 0 'Detect + Tier': compute size, type, domainFlags, risk tier, and the adjective-noun column-vs-table call ONCE at slice creation from data already on disk, with NO agent call on the happy path -- consumed by every downstream step instead of re-deriving risk per step, and data-driven so the tier cannot be gamed into FAST.

Lean-Adaptive's 3-tier granularity (TRIVIAL/STANDARD/CRITICAL) over a 2-tier model: a genuine TRIVIAL copy/config/style slice with no user-observable behavior stops at R0+R1 with NO behavior demo required, while D3's 2-tier FAST/FULL would still push it to the R2 behavior rung -- the finer tier saves a real round-trip on the highest-volume cheapest slices.

Lean-Adaptive's CRITICAL-tier NEGATIVE/adversarial probe: exercise the behavior with the spec-named boundary/empty/zero/duplicate input and confirm the spec-CORRECT result (not just the happy path) -- a concrete, named verification step targeting the confidently-wrong one-shot class (days_held=0 silently skips every trade, all tests green) that repetition-based stall detectors are blind to.

Quality-Gated-Lean's PROJECT_LANGUAGE.md reconciliation requirement enforced ON whichever step introduces a new durable noun (spec, slice, AND plan/build), not just at spec time -- closing the audit gap that planners and builders were never required to reconcile terms, the most concrete defense against domain-term drift across agents.

Quality-Gated-Lean's on-disk slices/ directory as the single source of truth, reconciled against the in-memory slice list on phase entry, so a resume after an obstruction-driven split or route-back is deterministic and idempotent -- the only design treating the renumbering/replay gap as a first-class step concern rather than a loop footnote.

The shared (all three) but cleanest-in-D3 honest verification ladder with the explicit test-tampering guard: the implementing agent NEVER has sole authority over the tests that gate it; a separate verifier diffs test files for tautology (always-true asserts, mocks returning the asserted value, hardcoded inputs/assertions mirroring current output rather than the spec example) and anchors every assertion to a spec-stated input->output example -- because >79% of agent cheating is test tampering.

The shared honesty contract 'verified' = the ONE observable behavior ran through its REAL entry point with REAL data (curl/DOM/static checks/code review are explicitly NOT this); when a rung is blocked the verdict degrades to 'code-verified' with the blocked check, replacement evidence, and named residual user-facing risk -- never a fabricated green proxy, and for irreversible/external actions verify via sandbox/dry-run/replay with the real-path gap logged as Accepted Risk.

ranking: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine

scores.approach: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
scores.note: Best overall balance. Honest verification ladder with concrete, testable Rung-3 surface checks (money sums exactly, idempotency replay = one side-effect, migration on realistic volume, malformed input -> typed error) that are the most actionable of the three. CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD forces a real run with a non-trivial threshold/count/filter param where output changes - an explicit mechanism against the days_held=0 silent-wrong class, the brief's hardest case. Keeps build (S4) as a discrete step, preserving one-hat discipline more cleanly than Design 1's fast-path merge. Adds operational determinism (on-disk slices/ dir as source of truth, reconciled on phase entry for deterministic resume-after-split) that Design 1 omits. Tradeoffs section is the most complete: modality limits, non-binary acceptance, reversibility SPOF, gameable fingerprints, human-oracle latency, acceptance-criteria genesis, and source-quality honesty all named. Loses a hair on efficiency vs Design 3 because Step-0 detection is not explicitly engine-side/agent-free.

scores.approach: Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
scores.note: Most aggressive lean (research+plan+build merged into ONE Step-3 round-trip for fast path) and the most rigorous verification articulation: explicit NEGATIVE/adversarial probe on CRITICAL slices for the confident-wrong class, plus a lightweight cross-slice COMPOSE check at every verify-build (not just phase end). Footprint/shared-surface tripwire is explicitly framed as harder to game than an error-hash window. Residual-risk section (a-e) is the most honest of the three. Edges Design 2 on raw step-count/efficiency but slightly behind on operational determinism (no on-disk slice reconciliation mentioned) and the inline build-on-fast-path means a fast-path build commits before any independent check - acceptable given low blast radius, but marginally less safe than keeping build discrete. Nearly tied with Design 2.

scores.approach: Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine
scores.note: Leanest framing - Step 0 (detect+tier+size+naming-lens) is engine-side with no agent call on the happy path, and FAST lane = effectively 3 agent calls. Best obstruction handling of the three: two-channel detection (declared architectural facts AND-gated with a coarse criteria-moved signal that target-perturbation cannot dodge) plus the strongest single anti-momentum rule anywhere - money/migrations/auth/external-effects are one-way BY RULE, never agent-judged, removing the reversibility classifier's single point of failure for the catastrophic cases. Revert-and-reset on Channel-B stalls. Marked down only on effectiveness: its defense against confident one-shot wrongness leans on R2 + spec-anchored assertions rather than an explicit forced non-trivial-parameter run, and it openly concedes this is its honest gap - whereas Designs 1 and 2 have a dedicated mechanism. That higher-severity class is the decisive axis, so it ranks third despite being lean and structurally elegant.

bestElementsToGraft: From SC-Strike: the HARD RULE that money/migrations/auth/external-effects are classified one-way (Tier 3) BY RULE, never by agent judgment - this removes the reversibility classifier as a single point of failure for exactly the catastrophic, irreversible cases, while keeping conservative-default for everything ambiguous.

From Quality-Gated-Lean (and SC-Strike): on-disk slices/ directory as the single source of truth, reconciled against the in-memory slice list on phase entry, so resume-after-a-split / route-back is deterministic on replay - the concrete operational fix for the renumbering/idempotency gap.

From Quality-Gated-Lean: the concrete, surface-specific Rung-3 verification checks (money parts sum EXACTLY + currency present + integer minor units; idempotency replay yields exactly ONE side-effect; migration run against realistic volume; malformed input returns a typed structured error not a 500) - the most testable, least-hand-wavy verification rung.

From Lean-Adaptive and Quality-Gated-Lean: the explicit CONFIDENT-WRONG / NEGATIVE-probe guard - force one real run with the spec-named boundary/zero/empty/duplicate or a non-trivial threshold value and confirm the output actually changes - the only defense against the silent days_held=0 class that repetition detectors are blind to.

From SC-Strike: the AND-gated stall detector - combine the {tool+target+error-hash} fingerprint with a coarse 'did any acceptance criterion flip FAIL->PASS in the window' signal, so a sunk-cost agent that perturbs the target to dodge the fingerprint still trips on 'made zero real progress.'

From Lean-Adaptive: the lightweight cross-slice COMPOSE check folded into every verify-build (confirm shared-surface contracts with prior slices are unbroken) so an integration mismatch surfaces at the slice, not at phase end.

From all three but cleanest in Lean-Adaptive: the mechanical adjective-noun lens driven by CARDINALITY x LIFECYCLE x EXCLUSIVITY with the automatic UNION-SMELL falsification test and a default of field-not-table, run at spec/slice/plan and re-checked at verify-build only on schema-touching slices.

From all three: REVERT-AND-RESET as the recovery primitive after 2 failed self-corrections (restore last-green, carry only a one-paragraph distilled lesson) instead of appending correction #3 to a polluted context - 'accumulated failed context is itself the cause of continued failure.'

From all three: surface-detection gating of canonical research AND Stripe-grade guardrails AND the heavy lens set, so a CSS/copy change pays zero domain ceremony - the named antidote to guardrail fatigue (a checklist that runs on everything gets skimmed on the one path that matters).

ranking: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine

scores.approach: Quality-Gated-Lean: Risk-Tiered Vertical Slicing with Loop-Owned Gates
scores.note: Strongest all-rounder. The CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD (force one real run with a non-trivial threshold/count/filter param where output changes) is applied at every verify-build, not just CRITICAL slices, giving broader coverage of the silent days_held=0/PID=0 class than D1's CRITICAL-only negative probe. Adds two operational details the others under-specify: on-disk slices/ reconciliation for deterministic resume-after-split, and explicit ship-safety/kill-switch at S6 closing the rollback gap. Conservative reversibility default plus the clearest modality tradeoff (Kafka/dbt/IaC degrade explicitly rather than mis-route). Honest about LLM-generated acceptance-criteria genesis as the deepest unmitigated dependency. Slightly heavier structured outputs than D3 but every field is load-bearing. Loses a hair on leanness vs D3's engine-side Step 0.

scores.approach: Lean-Adaptive Strike: Risk-Routed 6-Step Pipeline with a First-Class Architectural-Obstruction Loop
scores.note: Nearly tied for first. Best-articulated verification ladder (R0-R3 mapped per tier) and the original source of the NEGATIVE/adversarial probe aimed squarely at the confidently-wrong one-shot failure — but scopes it to CRITICAL only, so a confidently-wrong cheap slice can pass (it admits this in residual risk c). Best READ-BEFORE-WRITE framing and the footprint/shared-surface tripwire as a harder-to-game-than-error-hash trigger is excellent. Most intellectually honest tradeoffs section (residuals a-e, including concurrency/ownership it explicitly does not solve). Marginally behind D2 only because D2 generalizes the confident-wrong defense to all slices and adds resume-determinism + ship-safety that D1 mentions more lightly.

scores.approach: Self-Correcting Strike (SC-Strike): the Obstruction Loop is the Spine
scores.note: Leanest and most efficient: engine-side Step 0 with zero agent call on the happy path, FAST lane = 3 agent calls. Cleanest anti-gaming mechanism (Channel A declared + Channel B mechanical, AND-gated with criteria-movement so 'made zero progress' fires regardless of target perturbation) and the sharpest single mitigation of the reversibility single-point-of-failure: a HARD RULE that money/migrations/auth/external-effects are one-way by rule, never agent-judged. Keeps mandatory step-back/altitude (2-3 approaches) in Plan, which the others fold in less prominently. Ranked third only because its confident-wrong defense is the weakest of the three — it leans solely on the R2 behavior rung and spec-anchored assertions and openly names this as its honest gap, lacking the dedicated probe D1/D2 carry. Effectiveness dips slightly there.

bestElementsToGraft: From D3 (SC-Strike): the engine-side Step 0 that computes risk tier + domainFlags + adjective-noun call ONCE at slice creation with NO agent call on the happy path, so every downstream step's depth is data-driven and the FAST lane is just Plan->Build->Verify-Build (3 agent calls). This is the leanest realization of the risk-routing idea.

From D3 (SC-Strike): the AND-gated stall detector — Channel A (declared structured obstruction the engine corroborates against the diff: file outside footprint / shared foundation / new schema-contract / competing irreversible designs) plus Channel B (mechanical fingerprint counters) AND-gated with 'did any acceptance criterion flip FAIL->PASS in the window', so a target-perturbing sunk-cost agent cannot dodge 'made zero real progress.'

From D3 (SC-Strike): the HARD RULE removing agent taste from the single-point-of-failure classifier — money / migrations / auth / external-effects are one-way (Tier 3) by rule regardless of agent judgment; ambiguous defaults to one-way. This is the strongest mitigation of the reversibility-classifier risk shared across all three.

From D2 (Quality-Gated-Lean): the CONFIDENT-WRONG GUARD applied at EVERY verify-build (not just CRITICAL) — for any slice with a threshold/count/filter parameter, force one real run with a NON-trivial value where the output actually changes. This is the broadest concrete defense against the silent days_held=0 / PID=0 class that repetition detectors are blind to.

From D2 (Quality-Gated-Lean): on-disk slices/ directory as source of truth with reconciliation against the in-memory list on phase entry, making resume-after-a-split/route-back deterministic (closes the renumbering/idempotency gap the others mostly leave open).

From D2 (Quality-Gated-Lean) and D3: explicit SHIP-SAFETY / kill-switch check at the final/phase verify — confirm no half-built feature is live without a flag and any expand/contract migration is backout-safe; 'always-runnable' is not 'safe-to-ship.'

From D1 (Lean-Adaptive): the READ-BEFORE-WRITE codebase-grounding pass at the top of Plan (locate real entry points, existing utilities/validators/conventions, likely callers of shared code; do not invent a helper without grepping for an existing home) — kills duplication and missed-validation-layer defects at the source.

From D1 (Lean-Adaptive): the footprint/shared-surface tripwire as an obstruction trigger that is harder to game than an error-hash window — any edit reaching outside the slice footprint or modifying a file flagged shared fires the interrupt, checked from the diff not the agent's narration.

From D1 (Lean-Adaptive): the explicit R0-R3 verification ladder with rungs mapped to risk tier and the per-tier negative/adversarial probe for the highest-risk slices (exercise the spec-named boundary/empty/zero/duplicate input and confirm spec-correct, not just happy-path, behavior).

Shared across all three but cleanest in D2/D3: ADR + grep-able ARCH-DEBT(slice-id) recorded as CODE twice (never only in chat), with the NEXT slice's plan/surface-detect step grepping ARCH-DEBT touching its footprint BEFORE coding so the interim is visible to the next agent rather than silently built upon.

From D3 (SC-Strike): the mandatory one-paragraph STEP-BACK/altitude in Plan (name 2-3 distinct approaches with one-line tradeoffs, pick one, say why) as the cheap reverse gear, plus the REVERT-AND-RESET primitive on stall interrupts (discard polluted context, restore last green, restart carrying only a one-paragraph distilled lesson — never reach correction #3 in the same context).
